title: Implement RTCP Report Processing
description:
## Problem
RTCP packets not processed. MOS calculator uses placeholder values (jitter=0, delay=default). Quality metrics are guesses.

## Root Cause
- RTCP port allocated but socket immediately closed (media_session.ex:1557)
- SessionBin sends RTCP but no handler receives incoming RTCP
- MOS observer hardcodes jitter=0.0 (observer.ex:209)

## Files to Modify
- `/apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/media_session.ex` - Keep RTCP socket open
- `/apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/alaw_pipeline.ex` - Add RTCP sink element
- `/apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/opus_pipeline.ex` - Add RTCP sink element
- `/apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/observer.ex` - Remove placeholder values
- `/apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex` - Add RTCP metrics API

## Implementation (Elixir/Membrane Best Practices)

### Phase 1: RTCP Reception
1. Keep RTCP socket open in allocate_rtp_port():
```elixir
defp allocate_rtp_port(opts) do
  # ... existing port allocation ...
  # DON'T close rtcp_socket - keep it for receiving
  {:ok, rtp_socket, rtcp_socket, rtp_port}
end
```

2. Add RTCP Sink element to pipelines:
```elixir
child(:rtcp_sink, %ParrotMedia.RTCP.Sink{
  callback: &handle_rtcp_report/1
})
|> get_child(:rtp)
|> via_out(:rtcp_output)
|> get_child(:rtcp_sink)
```

### Phase 2: RTCP Parsing (Use ExRTCP)
ExRTCP already in deps. Parse incoming packets:
```elixir
defmodule ParrotMedia.RTCP.Sink do
  def handle_buffer(buffer, _ctx, state) do
    case ExRTCP.Packet.parse(buffer.payload) do
      {:ok, packets} ->
        Enum.each(packets, &process_rtcp_packet/1)
        {[], state}
      {:error, _} ->
        {[], state}
    end
  end
  
  defp process_rtcp_packet(%ExRTCP.Packet.ReceiverReport{reports: reports}) do
    Enum.each(reports, fn report ->
      jitter_ms = convert_jitter(report.jitter, clock_rate)
      rtt_ms = calculate_rtt(report.last_sr, report.delay)
      loss_pct = report.fraction_lost / 256.0 * 100
      
      MOS.Calculator.add_rtcp_metrics(calculator_pid, %{
        jitter_ms: jitter_ms,
        rtt_ms: rtt_ms,
        loss_percent: loss_pct
      })
    end)
  end
end
```

### Phase 3: MOS Calculator Integration
Add API for RTCP-sourced metrics (calculator.ex):
```elixir
def add_rtcp_metrics(pid, metrics) do
  GenServer.cast(pid, {:rtcp_metrics, metrics})
end

def handle_cast({:rtcp_metrics, metrics}, state) do
  # Merge RTCP metrics with interval data
  # RTCP provides more accurate jitter/delay than local estimation
  updated_interval = %{state.current_interval |
    jitter_ms: metrics.jitter_ms,
    delay_ms: metrics.rtt_ms / 2,  # One-way delay ≈ RTT/2
    rtcp_loss_percent: metrics.loss_percent
  }
  {:noreply, %{state | current_interval: updated_interval}}
end
```

## RTCP Report Block Fields (RFC 3550)
- fraction_lost: 8-bit (0-256 scale) → divide by 256 for percentage
- jitter: 32-bit RTP timestamp units → convert: jitter_ms = jitter / (clock_rate / 1000)
- last_sr + delay: Used for RTT calculation

## Test Strategy
- Unit test: Parse RTCP RR packet
- Integration test: Verify jitter/delay values flow to MOS calculator
- Verify MOS scores improve with real RTCP data vs placeholders

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RTCP socket kept open for receiving
- [ ] RTCP Sink element added to pipelines
- [ ] ExRTCP used to parse incoming RTCP
- [ ] RR block jitter extracted and converted to ms
- [ ] RTT calculated from LSR + DLSR
- [ ] MOS Calculator receives real jitter/delay values
- [ ] MOS scores reflect actual network conditions

title: INVITE retransmission race condition causes 488 rejection
description:
When receiving INVITE retransmissions (RFC 3261 mandated for UDP), the transaction layer has a race condition where the Registry lookup for an existing transaction can fail if the original transaction process has not yet completed registration.

**Reproduction Steps:**
1. Start B2BUA test server: SIP_TRACE=true LOG_LEVEL=debug mix run scripts/dev/test_b2bua_hold.exs
2. Call from pjsua: pjsua --null-audio --no-tcp 'sip:hold@127.0.0.1:5080;transport=udp'
3. Observe 488 Not Acceptable Here response

**Root Cause:**
1. First INVITE received at T+0 - transaction starting via DynamicSupervisor
2. Transaction registration via Registry happens during gen_statem init
3. INVITE retransmission arrives at T+500ms - sometimes BEFORE registration completes
4. find_server() returns :error even though transaction is starting
5. Second transaction created - tries to create MediaSession with same call_id
6. MediaSession creation fails with {:already_started, pid}
7. Bridge.Handler rejects call with 488

**Expected Behavior:**
Per RFC 3261 Section 17.2.1, INVITE retransmissions should be absorbed by the existing transaction and trigger retransmission of the last provisional response.

**Affected Component:**
ParrotSip.TransactionStatem - find_server() and transaction registration timing

title: Fix INVITE Retransmission Race Condition
description:
## Problem
RFC 3261 INVITE retransmissions cause 488 rejection due to race condition in transaction registration.

## Root Cause
Registry.register() happens in init_server_transaction/1 callback (line 1178) AFTER process spawn, not before. If retransmitted INVITE arrives during spawn window, find_server() returns :error, creating duplicate transaction.

## Files to Modify
- `/apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/transaction_statem.ex`
  - Lines 169-185: start_link/1 - add pre-registration via :via tuple
  - Line 1178: init_server_transaction - remove redundant Registry.register()
  - Lines 3096-3105: find_server/1 - should work after fix

## Implementation (Elixir/OTP Best Practices)
1. Use :via tuple in gen_statem.start_link/4 for atomic registration:
```elixir
def start_link(args) do
  transaction = extract_transaction(args)
  registry_key = {:via, Registry, {ParrotSip.Registry, transaction.id}}
  :gen_statem.start_link(registry_key, __MODULE__, args, [])
end
```

2. Remove redundant registration in init_server_transaction/2 (line 1178)

3. Ensure client transactions also use :via tuple pattern

## Test Strategy
- Add race condition test: send INVITE, immediately send retransmission (no sleep)
- Assert only 1 transaction created
- Verify retransmission absorbed and original response retransmitted

## RFC Reference
RFC 3261 Section 17.2.1: "If a retransmission of the request is received while in Proceeding state, the most recent provisional response MUST be retransmitted."

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Transaction registered atomically with process spawn via :via tuple
- [ ] Retransmissions routed to existing transaction immediately
- [ ] Race condition test passes
- [ ] All existing SIPp scenarios still pass

title: Fix WebSocket Source Address Bug
description:
## Problem
All WebSocket packets have source addresses 0.0.0.0:0. SIP routing/security depends on correct addresses.

## Root Cause
websocket_handler.ex:95-119 has broken implementations:
- get_peer_addr() calls :cowboy_req.peer(:cowboy_req) - passes atom instead of req map
- get_local_addr() calls Process.get(:socket) - socket never stored in process dictionary
- Both fall back to {{0,0,0,0}, 0}

## Files to Modify
- `/apps/parrot_transport/lib/parrot_transport/websocket_handler.ex`
  - Lines 40-50: init/2 - extract peer info here
  - Lines 54-60: websocket_init/1 - receive peer info from init
  - Lines 95-119: Remove broken get_peer_addr/get_local_addr

- `/apps/parrot_transport/test/parrot_transport/websocket_listener_test.exs`
  - Add address verification tests

## Implementation (Cowboy Best Practices)

### Fix: Extract peer in init/2, pass via state
```elixir
@impl :cowboy_websocket
def init(req, {handler, listener_pid}) do
  # Extract peer info while we have access to req
  {remote_ip, remote_port} = :cowboy_req.peer(req)
  
  # Get local address from listener (passed in opts)
  # OR from cowboy_req.sock/1 if available
  local_addr = get_local_from_listener(listener_pid)
  
  # Pass addresses through state tuple
  state = {handler, listener_pid, {remote_ip, remote_port}, local_addr}
  {:cowboy_websocket, req, state}
end

@impl :cowboy_websocket  
def websocket_init({handler, listener_pid, remote_addr, local_addr}) do
  send(listener_pid, {:connection_accepted, self()})
  
  state = %{
    handler: handler,
    remote_addr: remote_addr,
    local_addr: local_addr
  }
  {:ok, state}
end
```

### Cowboy API Reference
- :cowboy_req.peer(req) → {ip_tuple, port} - Available in init/2
- :cowboy_req.sock(req) → {ip_tuple, port} - Local socket address
- websocket_init/1 receives ONLY state, no req access

### Remove Broken Functions
Delete get_peer_addr/0 and get_local_addr/0 - no longer needed.

## Test Strategy
Add to websocket_listener_test.exs:
```elixir
test "includes correct peer address in incoming packet", %{port: port} do
  {:ok, client} = :gun.open({127, 0, 0, 1}, port)
  # ... setup WebSocket ...
  :gun.ws_send(client, stream_ref, {:text, "test"})
  
  assert_receive {:incoming_packet, %IncomingPacket{
    source: %Source{
      remote_addr: {{127, 0, 0, 1}, _port},
      local_addr: {{127, 0, 0, 1}, ^port}
    }
  }}
end
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Peer address extracted in init/2 using :cowboy_req.peer/1
- [ ] Local address obtained from listener or :cowboy_req.sock/1
- [ ] Addresses passed through state to websocket_init/1
- [ ] IncomingPacket contains correct remote_addr and local_addr
- [ ] Broken get_peer_addr/get_local_addr functions removed
- [ ] New test verifies address fields
- [ ] All existing WebSocket tests pass

title: Auth test format_auth_header fails due to map key ordering
description:
The tests in apps/parrot_sip/test/parrot_sip/auth_test.exs for format_auth_header/1 fail intermittently due to map key ordering:

```
Assertion with == failed
code:  assert header == ~s(Digest realm="atlanta.com", nonce="abc123")
left:  "Digest nonce=\"abc123\", realm=\"atlanta.com\""
right: "Digest realm=\"atlanta.com\", nonce=\"abc123\""
```

The test compares exact string equality but Elixir maps don't guarantee key order. The test should either:
1. Parse the formatted header and compare components
2. Use a sorted output in format_auth_header
3. Use a map-based comparison instead of string comparison

Affected tests:
- test format_auth_header/1 formats basic auth parameters
- test format_auth_header/1 excludes nil values
close_reason: Fixed in commit c4df2bd - sorted auth parameters for consistent ordering

title: MOS Scoring - Real-Time Call Quality Monitoring for parrot_media
description:
Implement MOS (Mean Opinion Score) scoring system for parrot_media that provides real-time call quality monitoring using the ITU-T G.107 E-model algorithm.

## Summary
- Real-time MOS calculation from RTP stream metrics (packet loss, jitter, delay)
- Observer + GenServer architecture for zero audio path latency
- Telemetry events for monitoring integration
- Handler behaviour for custom quality event callbacks
- Per-call quality summaries

## User Stories
1. US1 (P1): Real-Time Call Quality Monitoring - MOS scores during active calls
2. US2 (P2): Per-Call Quality Summary - Aggregate statistics at call end
3. US3 (P3): Quality Event Callbacks - Handler behaviour for external integration

## Key Design Decisions
- ITU-T G.107 E-model algorithm (network metrics, not audio analysis)
- Observer element in pipeline extracts RTP metadata (zero-copy, no latency)
- Separate GenServer for calculation (can crash without affecting audio)
- Telemetry events for observability
- Ephemeral data with event-driven persistence option

## Key Files (to create)
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos.ex (public API)
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/e_model.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/observer.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/handler.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/score.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/threshold.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/telemetry.ex

## Key Files (to modify)
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/application.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/media_session.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/alaw_pipeline.ex
- apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/opus_pipeline.ex

## Design Docs
- specs/005-mos-scoring/spec.md
- specs/005-mos-scoring/plan.md
- specs/005-mos-scoring/data-model.md
- specs/005-mos-scoring/research.md
- specs/005-mos-scoring/tasks.md

## Success Criteria
- SC-001: MOS calculated within 100ms of interval
- SC-002: <1% CPU overhead
- SC-003: Summary within 500ms of termination
- SC-004: 95% degradation detection
- SC-005: Callbacks within 50ms
- SC-006: MOS within 0.5 of reference values

title: Phase 2: Foundation - Core Data Structures
description:
Implement core data structures shared by all user stories. BLOCKING: All user stories depend on these structs.

## Goal
Define Score, Threshold, and Config structs that form the foundation of the MOS system.

## Context for Agents
This phase implements TDD-first data structures. Each struct gets tests written BEFORE implementation per CLAUDE.md TDD policy.

## Key Design (from data-model.md)
- Score struct: value (1.0-5.0), timestamp, metrics, quality_level
- Threshold struct: name, value, hysteresis, direction
- Config struct: interval_ms, thresholds, min_packets, default_delay

## Parallel Opportunities
- Score tests and implementation can parallel with Threshold
- Config can parallel with both

## Completion Criteria
- All struct tests pass
- All structs compile with proper typespecs
- Pattern matching helpers work correctly

title: Write tests for Score struct

```elixir
%ParrotMedia.MOS.Score{

  packet_loss_percent: float(),
  jitter_ms: float(),
  delay_ms: float(),
  r_factor: float(),

}
```

```elixir
  %Threshold{name: :good, value: 3.5},
  %Threshold{name: :fair, value: 3.0}
]
```

## Hysteresis Logic
A threshold is crossed when:
- previous_mos was on one side of (value +/- hysteresis)
- current_mos is on the other side

title: Phase 3: US1 - Real-Time Call Quality Monitoring (P1 MVP)
description:
Enable real-time MOS calculation during active calls. This is the P1 MVP delivering core functionality.

## User Story
As a platform operator, I want to monitor call quality in real-time during active calls so that I can identify and respond to quality degradation before calls fail.

## Independent Test
Initiate a call with varying network conditions and observe MOS scores update in real-time.

## Acceptance Scenarios
1. Stable audio → MOS 4.0-5.0 (excellent)
2. Packet loss → MOS decreases proportionally
3. High jitter → MOS reflects impact
4. MOS below threshold → quality alert emitted

## Components to Implement
1. E-Model Algorithm (ITU-T G.107)
   - calculate_mos/4, calculate_r_factor/4
   - r_to_mos/1, codec_params/1
   - calculate_id/1, calculate_ie_eff/3

2. Interval Management
   - Interval struct for metrics aggregation
   - Duration-based interval completion

3. Calculator GenServer
   - State transitions: awaiting_media → active → terminated
   - Metrics reception and aggregation
   - Threshold crossing detection

4. Pipeline Observer
   - Membrane Filter element
   - Zero-latency buffer pass-through
   - Stats request/response handling

5. Telemetry Events
   - [:parrot_media, :mos, :score]
   - [:parrot_media, :mos, :threshold_crossed]

## Design Docs
- specs/005-mos-scoring/research.md (E-model formula)
- specs/005-mos-scoring/data-model.md (structs)

## Completion Criteria
- MOS calculated every 5 seconds during active calls
- Threshold alerts emitted correctly
- Telemetry events working
- Integration test passes

title: US1.1: E-Model Algorithm Implementation
description:
Implement ITU-T G.107 E-model algorithm for MOS estimation.

## Context for Agents
The E-model calculates a rating factor R (0-100) from network metrics, then converts to MOS (1.0-5.0).

## Formula (from research.md)
R = R0 - Id - Ie_eff

Where:
- R0 = 93.2 (base quality)
- Id = delay impairment factor
- Ie_eff = equipment impairment with packet loss

## Functions to Implement
1. calculate_mos/4 - Main entry point
2. calculate_r_factor/4 - R-factor calculation
3. r_to_mos/1 - R to MOS conversion (ITU-T G.107 Appendix B)
4. calculate_id/1 - Delay impairment
5. calculate_ie_eff/3 - Equipment impairment with packet loss
6. codec_params/1 - Get Ie/Bpl for codec

## Codec Parameters (ITU-T G.113)
| Codec | Ie | Bpl |
|-------|-----|------|
| G.711 | 0 | 25.1 |
| Opus | 0 | 20.0 |

## TDD Approach
Write all tests first, then implement to make tests pass.

## Acceptance
- E-model produces MOS within 0.1 of reference values
- All codec parameters correct
- Edge cases handled (0% loss, 100% loss, high delay)

title: Write E-model R-factor calculation tests
description:
Write tests for E-model R-factor calculation BEFORE implementation.

## File
apps/parrot_media/test/parrot_media/mos/e_model_test.exs

## Test Cases
1. R-factor with 0% packet loss, 0 jitter, 50ms delay → ~93 (excellent)
2. R-factor with 5% packet loss → reduced R
3. R-factor with high jitter (100ms) → reduced R
4. R-factor with high delay (300ms) → reduced R
5. R-factor components are additive impairments

## Reference Values (from research.md)
- R = R0 - Id - Ie_eff
- R0 = 93.2

## Acceptance
- Tests created for calculate_r_factor/4
- Tests fail initially (red phase)

title: Implement EModel.calculate_ie_eff/3 (equipment impairment)
description:
Implement equipment impairment with packet loss.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/e_model.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
def calculate_ie_eff(ie, ppl, bpl) do
  ie + (95 - ie) * ppl / (ppl + bpl)
end
```

Where:
- ie = codec equipment impairment
- ppl = packet loss percentage (0-100)
- bpl = codec robustness factor

## Acceptance
- Ie_eff = Ie when ppl = 0
- Ie_eff approaches 95 as ppl → 100
- Bpl affects how quickly impairment increases

title: Write R-to-MOS conversion tests
description:
Write tests for R-factor to MOS conversion with reference values.

## File
apps/parrot_media/test/parrot_media/mos/e_model_test.exs

## Test Cases (ITU-T G.107 Appendix B)
1. R < 0 → MOS = 1.0
2. R > 100 → MOS = 4.5
3. R = 0 → MOS = 1.0
4. R = 50 → MOS ≈ 2.6
5. R = 70 → MOS ≈ 3.6
6. R = 93 → MOS ≈ 4.4

## Formula
```elixir
MOS = 1.0 + 0.035 * R + R * (R - 60) * (100 - R) * 7.0e-6
```

## Acceptance
- Tests verify known reference values
- Edge cases (R < 0, R > 100) handled

title: Write codec parameters tests
Write delay impairment (Id) calculation tests
description:
Write tests for delay impairment factor calculation.

## File
apps/parrot_media/test/parrot_media/mos/e_model_test.exs

## Test Cases (ITU-T G.107)
1. Delay <= 100ms → Id = 0.0 (no impairment)
2. Delay = 150ms → Id > 0 (some impairment)
3. Delay = 300ms → Id significantly higher
4. Delay = 500ms → Id approaches maximum

## Formula (for delay > 100ms)
```elixir
x = :math.log(ta / 100.0) / :math.log(2)
Id = 25.0 * (:math.pow(1 + :math.pow(x, 6), 1/6) -
       3 * :math.pow(1 + :math.pow(x / 3, 6), 1/6) + 2)
```

## Acceptance
- Id = 0 for delay <= 100ms
- Id increases logarithmically for higher delays

title: Implement EModel.calculate_mos/4
description:
Implement the main MOS calculation function.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/e_model.ex

## Function Signature
```elixir
@spec calculate_mos(float(), float(), float(), codec()) :: float()
def calculate_mos(packet_loss_percent, jitter_ms, delay_ms, codec \\ :g711)
```

## Implementation
1. Get codec parameters (Ie, Bpl) from codec_params/1
2. Calculate effective delay = delay_ms + (jitter_ms * 2)
3. Calculate Ie_eff = calculate_ie_eff(Ie, packet_loss_percent, Bpl)
4. Calculate Id = calculate_id(effective_delay)
5. Calculate R = 93.2 - Id - Ie_eff
6. Convert R to MOS using r_to_mos/1

## Acceptance
- Function works with all codec types
- Jitter is accounted for in effective delay
- Result is in 1.0-5.0 range

title: Implement EModel.calculate_r_factor/4
description:
Implement R-factor calculation (intermediate step).

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/e_model.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
@r0 93.2

def calculate_r_factor(packet_loss_percent, jitter_ms, delay_ms, codec \\ :g711) do
  {ie, bpl} = codec_params(codec)
  effective_delay = delay_ms + jitter_ms * 2
  ie_eff = calculate_ie_eff(ie, packet_loss_percent, bpl)
  id = calculate_id(effective_delay)
  @r0 - id - ie_eff
end
```

## Acceptance
- R-factor in 0-100 range (can go negative with extreme impairment)
- Matches expected values from tests

title: Implement EModel.r_to_mos/1
description:
Implement R-factor to MOS conversion per ITU-T G.107 Appendix B.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/e_model.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
def r_to_mos(r) when r < 0, do: 1.0
def r_to_mos(r) when r > 100, do: 4.5
def r_to_mos(r) do
  1.0 + 0.035 * r + r * (r - 60) * (100 - r) * 7.0e-6
end
```

## Acceptance
- Edge cases handled (R < 0, R > 100)
- Known reference values match

title: Implement EModel.codec_params/1
description:
Implement codec parameters lookup.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/e_model.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
@codec_params %{
  g711: {0, 25.1},
  g711_no_plc: {0, 4.3},
  opus: {0, 20.0},
  opus_low: {5, 15.0},
  g729: {11, 19.0}
}

def codec_params(codec) do
  Map.get(@codec_params, codec, @codec_params.g711)
end
```

## Acceptance
- All codec types return correct {Ie, Bpl}
- Unknown codec falls back to g711

title: Implement EModel.calculate_id/1 (delay impairment)
description:
Implement delay impairment factor calculation.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/e_model.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
def calculate_id(ta) when ta <= 100.0, do: 0.0
def calculate_id(ta) do
  x = :math.log(ta / 100.0) / :math.log(2)
  25.0 * (:math.pow(1 + :math.pow(x, 6), 1.0/6.0) -
         3 * :math.pow(1 + :math.pow(x / 3.0, 6), 1.0/6.0) + 2)
end
```

## Acceptance
- Id = 0 for delay <= 100ms
- Matches reference values for higher delays

title: US1.2: Interval Management
description:
Implement Interval struct for metrics aggregation over time windows.

## Context
The Interval struct aggregates RTP metrics (packets, loss, jitter) over a configurable time window (default 5 seconds). When the interval completes, a MOS score is calculated.

## Struct Definition
```elixir
%ParrotMedia.MOS.Interval{
  start_time: DateTime.t(),
  end_time: DateTime.t() | nil,
  duration_ms: non_neg_integer(),
  packets_received: non_neg_integer(),
  packets_expected: non_neg_integer(),
  packets_lost: non_neg_integer(),
  packet_loss_percent: float(),
  jitter_samples: [float()],
  jitter_ms: float(),
  delay_samples: [float()],
  delay_ms: float()
}
```

## Functions
- new/0 - Create new interval starting now
- add_metrics/2 - Add metrics from observer
- complete/1 - Mark interval as complete
- packet_loss_percent/1 - Calculate loss percentage
- avg_jitter/1 - Calculate average jitter

## Acceptance
- Interval correctly aggregates metrics
- Loss percentage calculated correctly
- Jitter averaged correctly

title: US1.3: Calculator GenServer
description:
Implement the MOS Calculator GenServer for metrics aggregation and score calculation.

## Context
The Calculator is the core component that:
- Receives metrics from Observer via messages
- Aggregates metrics over intervals
- Calculates MOS scores using E-model
- Detects threshold crossings
- Emits telemetry events

## State Machine
awaiting_media → active (on first metrics) → terminated (on session end)

## State Structure
```elixir
%{
  session_id: String.t(),
  config: Config.t(),
  status: :awaiting_media | :active | :terminated,
  current_interval: Interval.t() | nil,
  scores: [Score.t()],
  last_mos: float() | nil,
  handlers: [handler_ref()],
  threshold_states: %{atom() => :above | :below}
}
```

## Messages Handled
- {:metrics, metrics_map} - From Observer
- :interval_complete - Timer tick
- {:register_handler, handler} - Add callback handler
- :terminate - Session ending

## Dependencies
- E-Model (for MOS calculation)
- Interval (for aggregation)
- Threshold (for crossing detection)
- Telemetry (for event emission)

## Acceptance
- Calculator correctly aggregates metrics
- MOS calculated at interval boundaries
- Threshold crossing detected with hysteresis
- Telemetry events emitted

title: Implement Calculator GenServer init/start_link
description:
Implement Calculator GenServer initialization.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
use GenServer

def start_link(opts) do
  session_id = Keyword.fetch!(opts, :session_id)
  GenServer.start_link(__MODULE__, opts, name: via_tuple(session_id))
end

defp via_tuple(session_id) do
  {:via, Registry, {ParrotMedia.MOS.Registry, session_id}}
end

@impl true
def init(opts) do
  config = Config.merge(opts[:config] || %{})
  state = %{
    session_id: opts[:session_id],
    config: config,
    status: :awaiting_media,
    current_interval: nil,
    scores: [],
    last_mos: nil,
    handlers: [],
    threshold_states: init_threshold_states(config.thresholds),
    start_time: DateTime.utc_now()
  }
  {:ok, state}
end
```

## Acceptance
- GenServer starts with correct initial state
- Registered in MOS.Registry

title: Implement Calculator metrics reception (handle_cast)
description:
Implement Calculator metrics handling from Observer.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
@impl true
def handle_cast({:metrics, metrics}, %{status: :awaiting_media} = state) do
  # First metrics - transition to active and start interval
  interval = Interval.new()
  interval = Interval.add_metrics(interval, metrics)
  schedule_interval_timer(state.config.interval_ms)
  {:noreply, %{state | status: :active, current_interval: interval, media_started_at: DateTime.utc_now()}}
end

def handle_cast({:metrics, metrics}, %{status: :active, current_interval: interval} = state) do
  # Add metrics to current interval
  interval = Interval.add_metrics(interval, metrics)
  {:noreply, %{state | current_interval: interval}}
end
```

## Acceptance
- First metrics starts interval and timer
- Subsequent metrics aggregated into interval

title: Implement Calculator interval timer (handle_info)
description:
Implement Calculator interval completion and MOS calculation.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
@impl true
def handle_info(:interval_complete, %{status: :active} = state) do
  # Complete current interval
  interval = Interval.complete(state.current_interval)
  
  # Calculate MOS using E-model
  mos = EModel.calculate_mos(
    interval.packet_loss_percent,
    interval.jitter_ms,
    interval.delay_ms,
    state.config.codec
  )
  
  score = Score.new!(value: mos, timestamp: DateTime.utc_now(), ...)
  
  # Check thresholds
  state = check_thresholds(state, score)
  
  # Emit telemetry
  Telemetry.emit_score(state.session_id, score, interval)
  
  # Start new interval
  new_interval = Interval.new()
  schedule_interval_timer(state.config.interval_ms)
  
  {:noreply, %{state | 
    current_interval: new_interval, 
    scores: [score | state.scores],
    last_mos: mos
  }}
end

defp schedule_interval_timer(ms) do
  Process.send_after(self(), :interval_complete, ms)
end
```

## Acceptance
- MOS calculated at interval boundary
- Score added to list
- Telemetry emitted
- New interval started

title: Implement Calculator threshold crossing detection
description:
Implement threshold crossing detection with hysteresis.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
defp check_thresholds(%{last_mos: nil} = state, _score), do: state
defp check_thresholds(state, score) do
  Enum.reduce(state.config.thresholds, state, fn threshold, acc ->
    case Threshold.crossed?(threshold, state.last_mos, score.value) do
      {:crossed, direction} ->
        Telemetry.emit_threshold_crossed(state.session_id, score.value, state.last_mos, threshold, direction)
        notify_handlers(acc, {:threshold_crossed, threshold, direction, score})
        update_threshold_state(acc, threshold, direction)
      :not_crossed ->
        acc
    end
  end)
end
```

## Acceptance
- Threshold crossing detected
- Hysteresis prevents flapping
- Telemetry emitted on crossing

title: US1.4: Pipeline Observer Element
description:
Implement Membrane Filter element for RTP metrics collection.

## Context
The Observer is a Membrane Filter element that:
- Passes audio buffers through unchanged (zero latency)
- Periodically requests RTP stats from SessionBin
- Forwards metrics to Calculator via message

## Key Requirement: Zero Audio Path Latency
The observer MUST NOT add any latency to the audio path. It only reads metadata and forwards it asynchronously.

## Implementation Pattern
```elixir
use Membrane.Filter

def_input_pad(:input, flow_control: :auto, accepted_format: _any)
def_output_pad(:output, flow_control: :auto, accepted_format: _any)

# Pass buffers through unchanged
def handle_buffer(:input, buffer, _ctx, state) do
  {[buffer: {:output, buffer}], state}
end

# Request stats via timer
def handle_tick(:stats_timer, _ctx, state) do
  # Send StatsRequestEvent to RTP session
  # Forward stats to Calculator when received
end
```

## Dependencies
- Calculator (receives metrics)
- Membrane RTP plugin (provides stats)

## Acceptance
- Observer adds 0ms latency to audio
- Stats collected at configured interval
- Calculator receives metrics

title: Implement Observer as Membrane Filter
description:
Implement Observer Membrane Filter element.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/observer.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
defmodule ParrotMedia.MOS.Observer do
  use Membrane.Filter
  
  def_input_pad(:input, flow_control: :auto, accepted_format: _any)
  def_output_pad(:output, flow_control: :auto, accepted_format: _any)
  
  def_options(
    calculator_pid: [spec: pid(), description: "MOS Calculator process"],
    stats_interval_ms: [spec: pos_integer(), default: 1000]
  )
  
  @impl true
  def handle_init(_ctx, opts) do
    {[], %{calculator_pid: opts.calculator_pid, clock_rate: nil}}
  end
  
  @impl true
  def handle_playing(_ctx, state) do
    {[start_timer: {:stats_timer, state.stats_interval_ms}], state}
  end
  
  @impl true
  def handle_buffer(:input, buffer, _ctx, state) do
    # Pass through unchanged - ZERO LATENCY
    {[buffer: {:output, buffer}], state}
  end
  
  @impl true
  def handle_tick(:stats_timer, _ctx, state) do
    # Request stats from RTP session
    {[event: {:input, %Membrane.RTCP.ReceiverReport.StatsRequestEvent{}}], state}
  end
  
  @impl true
  def handle_event(:input, %Membrane.RTCP.ReceiverReport.StatsEvent{stats: stats}, _ctx, state) do
    metrics = convert_stats(stats, state.clock_rate)
    send(state.calculator_pid, {:metrics, metrics})
    {[], state}
  end
end
```

## Acceptance
- Buffers passed through with zero delay
- Stats requested and forwarded

title: Implement Telemetry module with emit functions
description:
Implement Telemetry module for MOS events.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/telemetry.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
defmodule ParrotMedia.MOS.Telemetry do
  @score_event [:parrot_media, :mos, :score]
  @threshold_event [:parrot_media, :mos, :threshold_crossed]
  @summary_event [:parrot_media, :mos, :call_summary]
  
  def emit_score(session_id, score, interval, opts \\ []) do
    :telemetry.execute(@score_event,
      %{
        mos_score: score.value,
        r_factor: score.r_factor,
        packet_loss_percent: score.packet_loss_percent,
        jitter_ms: score.jitter_ms,
        delay_ms: score.delay_ms,
        interval_duration_ms: interval.duration_ms
      },
      %{
        session_id: session_id,
        call_id: opts[:call_id],
        codec: opts[:codec],
        direction: opts[:direction]
      }
    )
  end
  
  def emit_threshold_crossed(...) do
    # Similar pattern
  end
  
  def events, do: [@score_event, @threshold_event, @summary_event]
end
```

## Acceptance
- All emit functions implemented
- Events match documented format

title: Phase 4: US2 - Per-Call Quality Summary (P2)
description:
Generate quality summary when calls end.

## User Story
As a platform operator, I want to receive a quality summary at the end of each call so that I can analyze call quality trends and identify problematic patterns.

## Independent Test

title: Implement CallSummary struct
description:
Implement CallSummary struct.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/call_summary.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
defmodule ParrotMedia.MOS.CallSummary do
  @enforce_keys [:session_id, :start_time]
  defstruct [
    :session_id, :call_id, :codec, :direction, :start_time,
    end_time: nil, duration_ms: 0,
    min_mos: nil, max_mos: nil, avg_mos: nil,
    total_packets: 0, total_lost: 0, overall_loss_percent: 0.0,
    quality_events: [], intervals_calculated: 0,
    status: :insufficient_data
  ]
  
  def from_calculator_state(state) do
    # Build summary from Calculator state
  end
  
  defp calculate_avg_mos(scores) when scores == [], do: nil
  defp calculate_avg_mos(scores) do
    Enum.sum(Enum.map(scores, & &1.value)) / length(scores)
  end
end
```

## Acceptance
- Struct implements all fields
- from_calculator_state/1 works correctly

```elixir
def handle_info(:terminate, state) do

end
```

title: Implement Handler behaviour definition
Implement handler registration in Calculator
description:
Add handler registration to Calculator.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
def handle_call({:register_handler, handler}, _from, state) do
  case init_handler(handler) do
    {:ok, handler_state} ->
      handlers = [{handler, handler_state} | state.handlers]
      {:reply, :ok, %{state | handlers: handlers}}
    {:error, reason} ->
      {:reply, {:error, reason}, state}
  end
end

defp init_handler({module, opts}) do
  module.init(opts)
end

defp init_handler(pid) when is_pid(pid) do
  {:ok, pid}
end
```

## Acceptance
- Handlers can be registered
- Module handlers initialized with init/1
- Process handlers stored as-is

title: Implement error-isolated handler invocation
description:
Implement error-isolated handler callbacks.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
defp notify_handlers(state, event) do
  Enum.reduce(state.handlers, [], fn handler, updated_handlers ->
    case safe_invoke_handler(handler, event, state.session_id) do
      {:ok, new_handler_state} ->
        [{elem(handler, 0), new_handler_state} | updated_handlers]
      {:error, reason} ->
        Logger.warning("Handler error: #{inspect(reason)}")
        updated_handlers  # Remove failed handler
    end
  end)
end

defp safe_invoke_handler({module, handler_state}, {:mos_score, score}, session_id) do
  try do
    module.handle_mos_score(session_id, score, handler_state)
  rescue
    e -> {:error, e}
  end
end
```

## Acceptance
- Handler errors caught and logged
- Other handlers still receive events
- Failed handlers removed

title: Implement ParrotMedia.MOS public API module
description:
Implement the public API module.

## File
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos.ex

## Implementation
```elixir
defmodule ParrotMedia.MOS do
  def start(session_id, opts \\ []) do
    Calculator.start_link([session_id: session_id] ++ opts)
  end
  
  def stop(session_id) do
    case lookup(session_id) do
      {:ok, pid} -> GenServer.call(pid, :terminate)
      error -> error
    end
  end
  
  def current_score(session_id) do
    GenServer.call(lookup!(session_id), :current_score)
  end
  
  def call_summary(session_id) do
    GenServer.call(lookup!(session_id), :call_summary)
  end
  
  def register_handler(session_id, handler) do
    GenServer.call(lookup!(session_id), {:register_handler, handler})
  end
  
  def enabled?(session_id) do
    case lookup(session_id) do
      {:ok, _} -> true
      _ -> false
    end
  end
  
  defp lookup(session_id) do
    case Registry.lookup(ParrotMedia.MOS.Registry, session_id) do
      [{pid, _}] -> {:ok, pid}
      [] -> {:error, :not_found}
    end
  end
end
```

## Acceptance
- All public API functions implemented
- Registry lookup works

title: Transport Layer: WebSocket and TLS Fixes
description:
## Overview
Fix transport layer bugs that break WebSocket and TLS functionality.

## Blocking Issues
1. WebSocket source addresses always return 0.0.0.0:0 (broken)
2. TLS/TCP architectural mismatch (TLS uses spawn_link, TCP uses gen_statem)
3. No outbound TLS client support
4. Duplicate UDP implementations (Udp vs UdpListener)

## Success Criteria
- WebSocket packets have correct source/remote addresses
- TLS connections use gen_statem like TCP
- Can make outbound TLS connections
- Single UDP implementation

## Technical Context
- websocket_handler.ex:95-119 has broken get_peer_addr/get_local_addr
- Cowboy peer info must be extracted in init/2, passed to websocket_init/1
- TLS connection.ex uses spawn_link loop instead of gen_statem

title: DSL Text-to-Speech Support
description:

- US2 (P1): TTS with DTMF collection say_prompt("text", max: 4)

- US6 (P3): Custom provider support

**File to modify**: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/call.ex

**Implementation**:
```elixir
@doc """
Plays synthesized speech from text.

## Examples

    call |> say("Your balance is $50")
    call |> say("Hello", profile: :premium)
"""
@spec say(t(), String.t()) :: t()
def say(%__MODULE__{} = call, text) when is_binary(text) do
  add_operation(call, {:say, text, []})
end

@spec say(t(), String.t(), keyword()) :: t()
def say(%__MODULE__{} = call, text, opts) when is_binary(text) and is_list(opts) do
  add_operation(call, {:say, text, opts})
end
```

title: T018: Add :say operation handling to ActionExecutor
description:
**Context for Agent**: Handle :say operations in the ActionExecutor.

**File to modify**: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex

**Implementation**:
```elixir
defp execute_operation({:say, text, opts}, call, context) do
  profile = Keyword.get(opts, :profile, Parrot.TTS.Config.default_profile())

  case Parrot.TTS.Synthesizer.get_audio(text, profile, opts) do
    {:ok, audio_file_path} ->
      # Delegate to existing play infrastructure
      execute_operation({:play, audio_file_path, opts}, call, context)

    {:error, reason} ->
      invoke_callback(:handle_tts_error, [text, reason], call, context)
  end
end
```

**Acceptance**: T015 tests pass.

**Depends on**: T017, T015 (tests first)
close_reason: T018 already implemented. 73 tests pass. Fixed MockMediaSession for gen_statem compatibility.

title: T020: Create basic profile config module
description:

**Implementation**:
```elixir
defmodule Parrot.TTS.Config do
  def default_profile do
    Application.get_env(:parrot, :tts, [])
    |> Keyword.get(:default_profile, :standard)
  end

  def get_profile(name) do
    profiles = Application.get_env(:parrot, :tts, [])
    |> Keyword.get(:profiles, [])

    Keyword.get(profiles, name, [])
  end

  def get_credentials(provider) do
    Application.get_env(:parrot, :tts, [])
    |> Keyword.get(:credentials, [])
    |> Keyword.get(provider, [])
    |> resolve_env_vars()
  end

  defp resolve_env_vars(config) do
    # Handle {:system, "VAR_NAME"} syntax
  end
end
```

title: T024: Add say_prompt/3 function to Call module
description:
**Context for Agent**: Add the say_prompt/3 function to Call module.

**File to modify**: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/call.ex

**Implementation**:
```elixir
@doc """
Plays synthesized speech then collects DTMF digits.

## Options

* :max - Maximum digits to collect (default: 20)
* :timeout - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30,000)
* :terminators - Digits that end collection (default: [])
* :profile - TTS provider profile to use

## Examples

    call |> say_prompt("Enter your PIN", max: 4, timeout: 10_000)
"""
@spec say_prompt(t(), String.t(), keyword()) :: t()
def say_prompt(%__MODULE__{} = call, text, opts \\ []) when is_binary(text) do
  call
  |> assign(:__pending_collect__, opts)
  |> say(text, Keyword.take(opts, [:profile, :voice]))
end
```

**Acceptance**: T022 tests pass.

**Depends on**: T022 (tests first), T017 (say/2 must exist)
close_reason: T024 already completed. say_prompt/3 exists in Call module. Commit: b63cc60

title: T038: Implement deterministic cache key generation
description:
**Context for Agent**: Implement SHA256-based cache key generation.

**File to modify**: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/tts/synthesizer.ex

**Implementation**:
```elixir
defp cache_key(text, config) do
  canonical = Jason.encode!(%{
    text: text,
    provider: config.provider,
    voice: config.voice,
    model: config.model,
    format: config.format
  })
  :crypto.hash(:sha256, canonical) |> Base.encode16(case: :lower)
end
```

**Reference**: specs/009-dsl-tts/research.md (cache key section)

**Acceptance**: T035 tests pass.

**Depends on**: T035
close_reason: T038 already implemented in Synthesizer. compute_cache_key/3 generates deterministic SHA256 keys.

title: Replace :sys.get_state with public APIs in tests
description:
Refactor 28 test files (205 usages) to use public query APIs instead of :sys.get_state.

## Problem
Tests use :sys.get_state to inspect internal process state, coupling tests to implementation details.

## Solution
1. Where public API exists: Replace :sys.get_state with the public API
2. Where no API exists: Add a :get_state or :get_status handler to the module

## Files by App
- parrot_media: 16 files (82 usages)
- parrot_sip: 7 files (112 usages)  
- parrot_transport: 5 files (9 usages)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All :sys.get_state calls replaced
- All tests still pass
- No new :sys.get_state usage introduced

title: [CODE-REVIEW] GenServer.cast for State Changes Needing Acknowledgment
description:
## Problem
**Location:** `apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/ua.ex` lines 113, 136, 239

State changes use `GenServer.cast` instead of `call`, creating race conditions.

## Code
```elixir
def answer(_ua, %Entity{...} = entity, opts \\ []) do
  # ... send SIP response ...
  GenServer.cast(ua_pid, {:entity_state_change, entity.id, :confirmed})  # No ack!
  :ok
end
```

## Impact
- Caller thinks state is updated, but message may be delayed/lost
- Race conditions on follow-up BYE routing
- Hard to debug state inconsistencies

## Fix
Use `GenServer.call` with timeout for state changes:
```elixir
def answer(_ua, %Entity{...} = entity, opts \\ []) do
  # ... send SIP response ...
  :ok = GenServer.call(ua_pid, {:entity_state_change, entity.id, :confirmed}, 5000)
  :ok
end
```

## Severity
P2 - Tech debt causing potential call handling issues

title: Unify TLS Connection to gen_statem Pattern
description:
## Problem
TLS connections use spawn_link loop (lines 357-430), while TCP uses gen_statem. Architectural inconsistency makes debugging impossible.

## Root Cause
connection.ex has two patterns:
- TCP: Uses gen_statem with proper states (:connecting, :connected, :reconnecting)
- TLS: Uses spawn_link with manual message loop (tls_connection_loop/6)

## Files to Modify
- `/apps/parrot_transport/lib/parrot_transport/connection.ex`
  - Lines 357-430: Replace tls_connection_loop with gen_statem
  - Lines 135-180: Unify inbound connection handling

## Implementation (OTP Best Practices)

### Convert TLS to gen_statem
```elixir
# TLS should use same state machine as TCP
def start_tls(ssl_socket, remote_addr, local_addr, initial_data) do
  config = %{
    transport: :tls,
    socket: ssl_socket,
    remote_addr: remote_addr,
    local_addr: local_addr
  }
  
  # Use gen_statem like TCP
  :gen_statem.start_link(__MODULE__, {:tls_inbound, config, initial_data}, [])
end

# Add TLS state functions
def connected(:info, {:ssl, socket, data}, %{transport: :tls} = state) do
  handle_incoming_data(data, state)
end

def connected(:info, {:ssl_closed, _socket}, state) do
  {:next_state, :stopping, state}
end

def connected(:info, {:ssl_error, _socket, reason}, state) do
  Logger.error("TLS error: #{inspect(reason)}")
  {:next_state, :stopping, state}
end
```

### Benefits of gen_statem for TLS
1. Query connection state via :gen_statem.call
2. Graceful shutdown with proper state transitions
3. Consistent API with TCP connections
4. Better error handling and supervision
5. Telemetry/logging integration

### Add TLS-specific Handling
- :ssl.send vs :gen_tcp.send
- :ssl.close vs :gen_tcp.close
- :ssl_closed vs :tcp_closed message patterns

## Test Strategy
- Add TLS connection state query test
- Verify graceful shutdown works
- Test reconnection (if applicable)
- Verify ContentLength framing works over TLS

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TLS connections use gen_statem, not spawn_link
- [ ] Same state machine states as TCP
- [ ] Can query TLS connection state
- [ ] Graceful shutdown works
- [ ] :ssl message patterns handled correctly
- [ ] All TLS tests pass
- [ ] No spawn_link loop code remains

title: [test-infrastructure] Wrapper scripts have timing/process management issues

comments.author: byoungdale
comments.text:
## Issue Description

The test wrapper scripts (test_and_report.sh / run_test.sh) have timing/process management issues:

1. **SIGTERM sent prematurely** - Server processes receive SIGTERM 0.5-1.5 seconds after startup, before the 3-second wait period completes

2. **Inconsistent behavior** - Some tests pass (test_answer_play, test_hangup_dsl, test_reject_dsl, test_sdp_negotiation) while others fail (test_dtmf_dsl) with same infrastructure

3. **Manual runs work** - Running tests manually outside the wrapper scripts works correctly

4. **orchestrator_lock.sh bug** - Uses unsupported `--assignee` flag with `bd q` command

## Root Cause Hypothesis

When `run_test.sh` is called via subshell `$(...)` in `test_and_report.sh`, the `nohup` process may not be properly detached from the subshell's process group. When the subshell exits, children may receive signals.

## Workaround

Tests can be run manually or with `SKIP_LOCK=1` and longer delays between tests.

## To Fix

1. Use `setsid` or `disown` in addition to `nohup`
2. Add `set +e` before background process spawning
3. Fix orchestrator_lock.sh to not use `--assignee` flag

title: Complete Mini PBX Example Application
description:
# Complete Mini PBX Example Application

Build the full Mini PBX example demonstrating all DSL features working together in a realistic application.

## Business Value
- Proves DSL is production-ready
- Documentation by example
- Reference implementation for users
- Integration test for all features

## Features to Implement

### 1. Extensions (1xxx)
```elixir
def handle_invite(%{to: "sip:1" <> ext <> "@" <> _} = invite) do
  invite
  |> answer()
  |> bridge("sip:#{ext}@internal", timeout: 30_000)
end

def handle_bridge_complete({:failed, :no_answer}, call) do
  call |> forward_to_voicemail(call.assigns.extension)
end
```

### 2. Voicemail System
- Record greeting on first setup
- Play greeting, record message
- Store with metadata (from, timestamp, duration)
- MWI (Message Waiting Indicator) via NOTIFY

### 3. Auto-Attendant IVR
```elixir
def handle_invite(%{to: "sip:100@" <> _} = invite) do
  invite
  |> answer()
  |> play("welcome.wav")
  |> prompt("main-menu.wav", collect: [max: 1, timeout: 5_000])
end

def handle_dtmf("1", call), do: call |> bridge("sip:sales@internal")
def handle_dtmf("2", call), do: call |> bridge("sip:support@internal")
def handle_dtmf("0", call), do: call |> bridge("sip:operator@internal")
```

### 4. Outbound PSTN with Failover
```elixir
def handle_invite(%{to: "sip:9" <> number <> "@" <> _} = invite) do
  invite
  |> answer()
  |> fork([
    {"sip:#{number}@carrier1.com", timeout: 10_000},
    {"sip:#{number}@carrier2.com", timeout: 10_000}
  ], strategy: :first_answer)
end
```

### 5. Presence Integration
- Show extension status (available/busy/offline)
- Update on call start/end
- BLF (Busy Lamp Field) support

### 6. Registration
- Digest authentication
- Multiple devices per extension
- Expiry handling

## File Structure

```
apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/
├── router.ex           # Main router
├── extensions.ex       # Extension handling
├── voicemail.ex        # Voicemail handler
├── auto_attendant.ex   # IVR handler
├── outbound.ex         # PSTN routing
├── presence.ex         # Presence handler
├── registration.ex     # Registration handler
└── storage.ex          # ETS-based storage
```

## Testing
- Unit tests for each handler
- Integration test with full call flows
- SIPp scenarios for each feature

## Dependencies
- Requires: All DSL features implemented
- Should work with: Real SIP phones for demo

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Extensions dial each other
- [ ] Voicemail records and plays back
- [ ] IVR navigates correctly
- [ ] Outbound calls work with failover
- [ ] Presence updates in real-time
- [ ] Registration with auth works
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Works with real SIP phones

title: Create Mini PBX router and structure
description:
Set up the Mini PBX example application structure.

Directory: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/

```elixir
# router.ex
defmodule Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Router do
  use Parrot.Router

  pipeline :authenticated do
    plug :verify_registration
  end

  # Internal extensions
  scope "/", from_ip: "192.168.0.0/16" do
    pipe_through :authenticated
    invite "1xxx", Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Extensions
    invite "9xxx", Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Outbound
  end

  # Auto-attendant
  invite "100", Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.AutoAttendant

  register Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Registration
  presence Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Presence
end
```

Files to create:
- router.ex - Main router
- storage.ex - ETS-based storage for demo
- config.ex - Configuration helpers

Acceptance:
- Router compiles and routes correctly
- ETS storage for registrations/voicemail
- Basic configuration for extensions
- Unit tests for routing

title: Implement Extensions handler
description:
Create extension-to-extension calling in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/extensions.ex

```elixir
defmodule Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Extensions do
  use Parrot.InviteHandler

  alias Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.{Storage, Voicemail}

  def handle_invite(%{to: "sip:1" <> ext <> "@" <> _} = invite) do
    # Look up extension registration
    case Storage.get_registration(ext) do
      {:ok, contact} ->
        invite
        |> answer()
        |> assign(:extension, ext)
        |> assign(:caller, get_caller(invite))
        |> bridge(contact, timeout: 30_000)

      {:error, :not_found} ->
        invite |> reject(404)
    end
  end

  def handle_bridge_complete(:answered, call) do
    Presence.notify(call.assigns.extension, :busy)
    {:noreply, call}
  end

  def handle_bridge_complete({:failed, :no_answer}, call) do
    call |> forward_to_voicemail()
  end

  def handle_bridge_complete({:failed, :busy}, call) do
    call |> play("extension-busy.wav") |> hangup()
  end

  def handle_hangup(call) do
    Presence.notify(call.assigns.extension, :available)
    {:noreply, call}
  end

  defp forward_to_voicemail(call) do
    call
    |> assign(:voicemail, true)
    |> play(Voicemail.get_greeting(call.assigns.extension))
  end
end
```

Acceptance:
- Extensions can call each other
- No answer -> voicemail
- Busy -> busy message
- Presence updated on call start/end
- Unit tests

title: Implement Voicemail system
description:
Create voicemail functionality in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/voicemail.ex

```elixir
defmodule Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Voicemail do
  use Parrot.InviteHandler

  alias Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Storage

  # Called when forwarded from Extensions handler
  def handle_play_complete(greeting, call) when call.assigns.voicemail do
    call
    |> play("leave-message-after-beep.wav")
  end

  def handle_play_complete("leave-message-after-beep.wav", call) do
    call
    |> record(voicemail_path(call), max_duration: 120_000, beep: true)
  end

  def handle_record_complete(filename, duration, call) do
    # Store voicemail metadata
    Storage.save_voicemail(%{
      extension: call.assigns.extension,
      from: call.assigns.caller,
      file: filename,
      duration: duration,
      timestamp: DateTime.utc_now()
    })

    # Send MWI notification
    notify_mwi(call.assigns.extension)

    call |> play("message-saved.wav") |> hangup()
  end

  # Voicemail retrieval (dial *86)
  def handle_invite(%{to: "sip:*86@" <> _} = invite) do
    invite
    |> answer()
    |> prompt("enter-pin.wav", collect: [max: 4, timeout: 10_000])
  end

  def handle_dtmf(pin, call) when is_binary(pin) do
    if verify_pin(call.assigns.extension, pin) do
      play_voicemails(call)
    else
      call |> play("invalid-pin.wav") |> hangup()
    end
  end
end
```

title: Implement Auto-Attendant IVR
description:
Create IVR auto-attendant in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/auto_attendant.ex

```elixir
defmodule Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.AutoAttendant do
  use Parrot.InviteHandler

  def handle_invite(invite) do
    invite
    |> answer()
    |> assign(:menu, :main)
    |> assign(:retries, 0)
    |> play("welcome.wav")
  end

  def handle_play_complete("welcome.wav", call) do
    call |> prompt("main-menu.wav", collect: [max: 1, timeout: 5_000])
  end

  # Main menu options
  def handle_dtmf("1", %{assigns: %{menu: :main}} = call) do
    call |> bridge("sip:sales@internal", timeout: 30_000)
  end

  def handle_dtmf("2", %{assigns: %{menu: :main}} = call) do
    call |> bridge("sip:support@internal", timeout: 30_000)
  end

  def handle_dtmf("0", %{assigns: %{menu: :main}} = call) do
    call |> bridge("sip:operator@internal", timeout: 30_000)
  end

  def handle_dtmf(:timeout, call) do
    if call.assigns.retries < 2 do
      call
      |> assign(:retries, call.assigns.retries + 1)
      |> play("sorry-try-again.wav")
    else
      call |> play("goodbye.wav")
    end
  end

  def handle_play_complete("sorry-try-again.wav", call) do
    call |> prompt("main-menu.wav", collect: [max: 1, timeout: 5_000])
  end

  def handle_play_complete("goodbye.wav", call) do
    call |> hangup()
  end

  def handle_bridge_complete({:failed, _reason}, call) do
    call |> play("all-operators-busy.wav") |> hangup()
  end
end
```

Acceptance:
- Welcome message plays
- Menu options work (1=sales, 2=support, 0=operator)
- Timeout retries (max 2)
- Bridge to departments
- Handle bridge failures
- Unit tests for menu flow

title: Implement Outbound PSTN routing with failover
description:
Create outbound calling with carrier failover in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/outbound.ex

```elixir
defmodule Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Outbound do
  use Parrot.InviteHandler

  @carriers [
    {"carrier1.example.com", priority: 1, weight: 10},
    {"carrier2.example.com", priority: 2, weight: 5}
  ]

  def handle_invite(%{to: "sip:9" <> number <> "@" <> _} = invite) do
    # Validate number format
    case validate_number(number) do
      {:ok, normalized} ->
        invite
        |> answer()
        |> assign(:dialed_number, normalized)
        |> fork(build_destinations(normalized), 
            strategy: :first_answer, 
            timeout: 30_000)

      {:error, :invalid} ->
        invite |> reject(404)
    end
  end

  def handle_fork_complete(:answered, call) do
    # Call connected to carrier
    {:noreply, call}
  end

  def handle_fork_complete({:failed, :all_failed}, call) do
    call |> play("call-cannot-be-completed.wav") |> hangup()
  end

  defp build_destinations(number) do
    Enum.map(@carriers, fn {host, _opts} ->
      {"sip:#{number}@#{host}", timeout: 10_000}
    end)
  end

  defp validate_number(number) do
    # E.164 validation
    if Regex.match?(~r/^\d{10,15}$/, number) do
      {:ok, number}
    else
      {:error, :invalid}
    end
  end
end
```

Features:
- Dial 9 + number for outside line
- Multiple carriers with priority
- First-answer strategy
- All-failed handling
- Number validation

Acceptance:
- 9xxx routes to PSTN
- Failover to second carrier
- Invalid numbers rejected
- All carriers fail -> error message
- Unit tests

title: Implement Registration handler with auth
description:
Create registration handler in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/registration.ex

```elixir
defmodule Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Registration do
  use Parrot.RegistrationHandler

  alias Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Storage

  @impl true
  def get_password(username) do
    # Look up in storage (for demo, could be hardcoded)
    case Storage.get_user(username) do
      {:ok, user} -> {:ok, user.password}
      {:error, :not_found} -> {:error, :user_not_found}
    end
  end

  @impl true
  def store_binding(aor, contact, expires) do
    Storage.save_registration(%{
      aor: aor,
      contact: contact,
      expires: expires,
      registered_at: DateTime.utc_now()
    })

    # Update presence
    Parrot.Presence.notify(aor, %{status: :available})
    :ok
  end

  @impl true
  def get_bindings(aor) do
    Storage.get_registrations(aor)
  end

  @impl true
  def handle_registration_expired(aor, contact) do
    Storage.delete_registration(aor, contact)
    Parrot.Presence.notify(aor, %{status: :offline})
    :ok
  end
end
```

Features:
- Digest authentication
- Multiple contacts per extension
- Expiry handling
- Presence integration

Acceptance:
- Registration with auth works
- Multiple devices per extension
- Expiry cleans up
- Presence updates on register/expire

title: Implement Presence handler
description:
Create presence handler in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/mini_pbx/presence.ex

```elixir
defmodule Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Presence do
  use Parrot.PresenceHandler

  alias Parrot.Examples.MiniPBX.Storage

  @impl true
  def authorize_subscription(watcher, presentity) do
    # For demo, allow all internal subscriptions
    :allow
  end

  @impl true
  def store_subscription(subscription) do
    Storage.save_subscription(subscription)
    :ok
  end

  @impl true
  def get_subscriptions(presentity) do
    Storage.get_subscriptions(presentity)
  end

  @impl true
  def get_presence(presentity) do
    case Storage.get_presence_state(presentity) do
      {:ok, :available} -> %{status: :open, note: "Available"}
      {:ok, :busy} -> %{status: :closed, note: "On a call"}
      {:ok, :dnd} -> %{status: :closed, note: "Do not disturb"}
      _ -> %{status: :closed, note: "Offline"}
    end
  end

  @impl true
  def handle_publish(presentity, presence_state) do
    Storage.set_presence_state(presentity, presence_state.status)
    :ok
  end
end
```

Features:
- Extension status (available/busy/offline)
- Subscription authorization
- BLF (Busy Lamp Field) support via NOTIFY

Acceptance:
- Subscribe to extension presence
- Get NOTIFY on status change
- Publish own status

title: [CODE-REVIEW] GitHub Dependency Should Use Hex.pm
description:
## Problem
**Location:** `apps/parrot_media/mix.exs:37-38`

A custom fork is pulled from GitHub instead of Hex.pm:
```elixir
{:membrane_rtp_g711_plugin,
 github: "byoungdale/membrane_rtp_g711_plugin", branch: "byoungdale/update-rtp-format"},
```

## Impact
- GitHub deps are fragile (branch deletion, repo removal)
- No version pinning - any commit can change behavior
- Slower CI (clone vs. download)
- Reproducible builds harder

## Fix Options
1. **Publish to Hex.pm** - Work with maintainer to merge changes
2. **Pin to git SHA** - `ref: "abc123"` for stability
3. **Vendor locally** - If project-specific fork

## Severity
P2 - Build stability and reproducibility concern

title: Update RegistrationHandler behaviour for richer binding data
description:
The current get_bindings/1 callback in Parrot.RegistrationHandler returns just contact URIs. Update it to return richer data that includes expiration information.

## Current behaviour callback (apps/parrot/lib/parrot/registration_handler.ex)
```elixir
@callback get_bindings(aor :: String.t()) :: [String.t()]
```

## Proposed change
```elixir
@callback get_bindings(aor :: String.t()) :: [binding()]
@type binding :: %{contact: String.t(), expires: non_neg_integer(), registered_at: non_neg_integer()}
```

## Files to modify
1. apps/parrot/lib/parrot/registration_handler.ex - Update @callback and @type specs
2. apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/registrar.ex - Update Handler.get_bindings/1 implementation to return full binding maps

## Consideration
The expires value could be:
- Original expires value from registration (simpler)
- Remaining seconds calculated from registered_at + expires - now (more accurate per RFC)

Recommend returning the full binding map so build_contact_headers/1 can calculate remaining time accurately.

title: Fix B2BUA State Synchronization
description:
## Problem
DSL answer() doesn't update B2BUA A-leg state. B2BUA.connect() fails with :leg_not_answered because leg remains in :trying state.

## Root Cause
ActionExecutor.execute_answer/3 updates call.state to :answered but doesn't notify B2BUA. B2BUA.connect() requires both legs in :answered state.

## Files to Modify
- `/apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex`
  - Lines 126-168: execute_answer/3 - add B2BUA notification

- `/apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/handler.ex`
  - Lines 199-206: process_invite_with_media context - add b2bua_pid

- `/apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/b2bua.ex`
  - Lines 462-482: connect/3 - already checks leg state correctly

## Implementation (Elixir Best Practices)
1. Pass b2bua_pid in context (handler.ex):
```elixir
context = %{
  uas: uas,
  sip_msg: req_sip_msg,
  media_pid: media_pid,
  dialog_id: req_sip_msg.call_id,
  sdp_answer: sdp_answer,
  response_fn: Map.get(args, :response_fn),
  b2bua_pid: Map.get(args, :b2bua_pid)  # ADD THIS
}
```

2. Notify B2BUA in execute_answer/3 (action_executor.ex):
```elixir
def execute_answer(call, %{sip_msg: sip_msg} = context, _opts) do
  # ... existing answer logic ...
  
  # Notify B2BUA of A-leg state change
  if b2bua_pid = Map.get(context, :b2bua_pid) do
    sdp = Map.get(context, :sdp_answer)
    B2BUA.update_leg_state(b2bua_pid, :a_leg, :answered, %{sdp: sdp})
  end
  
  updated_call = %{call | state: :answered, __dialog_id__: dialog_id}
  {:ok, updated_call}
end
```

3. Add update_leg_state/4 API to B2BUA if not exists

## Test Strategy
- Unskip B2BUA tests in test/sipp/b2bua_test.exs
- Test: A-leg INVITE → answer() → verify leg state is :answered
- Test: A-leg answer() → originate B-leg → B-leg answers → connect succeeds

## Leg State Machine Reference
Valid transitions (from Parrot.Leg):
- init → trying → ringing → answered → terminated
- answered → held → answered

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] b2bua_pid passed through context to ActionExecutor
- [ ] execute_answer notifies B2BUA of :answered state
- [ ] B2BUA.connect() succeeds after both legs answered
- [ ] Unskip and pass B2BUA SIPp tests
- [ ] All 6 skipped B2BUA scenarios pass

title: Remove Duplicate UDP Implementation
description:
## Problem
Two UDP implementations exist: Udp (GenServer) and UdpListener (gen_statem). Maintenance burden and confusion.

## Files
- `/apps/parrot_transport/lib/parrot_transport/udp.ex` - Legacy GenServer (196 lines)
- `/apps/parrot_transport/lib/parrot_transport/udp_listener.ex` - Modern gen_statem (266 lines)

## Analysis
UdpListener (gen_statem) is the preferred implementation:
- Proper state machine with :bound and :stopping states
- Comprehensive test coverage (105+ lines)
- Matches TCP/TLS patterns

Udp (GenServer) appears to be legacy:
- Simple GenServer pattern
- No dedicated tests
- Different API (handler registration)

## Implementation
1. Search codebase for Udp module usage:
```bash
grep -r "ParrotTransport.Udp\." --include="*.ex"
grep -r "Udp.start_link" --include="*.ex"
```

2. If Udp is unused:
   - Delete udp.ex
   - Update module docs if referenced

3. If Udp is used:
   - Migrate callers to UdpListener
   - Add deprecation warning
   - Delete after migration confirmed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Determine which UDP implementation is active
- [ ] Migrate any Udp users to UdpListener
- [ ] Delete unused implementation
- [ ] Single UDP implementation remains
- [ ] All tests pass

title: Implement REGISTER Digest Authentication
description:
## Problem
REGISTER authentication is skipped. Bridge.Handler:362-363 has comment "skip authentication". Security vulnerability.

## Root Cause
process_registration/4 doesn't use existing Registrar module with auth support. Just calls handler callbacks directly.

## Existing Infrastructure (Ready to Use)
- ParrotSip.Auth - Full Digest MD5/MD5-sess support
- ParrotSip.Registrar - Challenge/response flow implemented
- ParrotSip.Auth.NonceStore - Nonce generation and replay prevention
- ParrotSip.RegistrationHandler - Behaviour with get_password/1 callback

## Files to Modify
- `/apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/handler.ex`
  - Lines 362-397: Replace skip-auth with Registrar.process_register/4

- `/apps/parrot/lib/parrot/application.ex`
  - Start NonceStore in supervision tree

## Implementation (Security Best Practices)

### 1. Start NonceStore in Supervision Tree
```elixir
# application.ex
children = [
  # ... existing children ...
  {ParrotSip.Auth.NonceStore, name: Parrot.NonceStore, ttl: 300}
]
```

### 2. Use Registrar in Bridge.Handler
```elixir
defp process_registration(req_sip_msg, uas, handler_module, args) do
  realm = Application.get_env(:parrot, :sip_realm, "parrot")
  nonce_store = Process.whereis(Parrot.NonceStore)
  
  case ParrotSip.Registrar.process_register(req_sip_msg, handler_module, realm, nonce_store) do
    {:ok, response} ->
      # Authentication succeeded, binding stored
      send_response(uas, response)
      
    {:challenge, response} ->
      # No auth header or invalid - send 401 with WWW-Authenticate
      send_response(uas, response)
      
    {:error, response} ->
      # Auth failed - send 403 Forbidden
      send_response(uas, response)
  end
end
```

### 3. Handler Must Implement get_password/1
```elixir
defmodule MyRegistrar do
  use Parrot.RegistrationHandler
  
  @impl true
  def get_password(username) do
    case lookup_user(username) do
      {:ok, user} -> {:ok, user.password}
      :error -> :error
    end
  end
  
  @impl true
  def authenticate(credentials) do
    # Additional checks beyond password (IP whitelist, etc.)
    :ok
  end
  
  # ... store_binding, get_bindings ...
end
```

## RFC 3261 Flow
1. REGISTER without Authorization → 401 Unauthorized + WWW-Authenticate
2. REGISTER with Authorization → Validate digest → 200 OK or 403 Forbidden

## Test Strategy
Existing SIPp scenarios in test/sipp/register_auth_test.exs:
- uac_register_auth.xml - Challenge/response flow
- uac_register_auth_wrong_password.xml - 403 on bad password

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] NonceStore started in supervision tree
- [ ] Bridge.Handler uses Registrar.process_register/4
- [ ] 401 challenge sent when no Authorization header
- [ ] Digest credentials validated correctly
- [ ] 403 sent on wrong password
- [ ] 200 OK sent on successful auth
- [ ] Nonce replay prevention works
- [ ] Remove "skip authentication" comment
- [ ] SIPp register_auth tests pass

title: SIP UPDATE Message for Mid-Call Media Changes
description:
# SIP UPDATE Message for Mid-Call Media Changes

Implement RFC 3311 UPDATE method for mid-call session modifications without affecting dialog state.

## Business Value
- Smoother hold/resume (no re-INVITE delays)
- Dynamic codec switching for network adaptation
- Required for WebRTC interoperability (ICE restarts)
- RFC compliance for enterprise SIP interop

## UPDATE vs re-INVITE (RFC 3311)

| Aspect | UPDATE | re-INVITE |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| Dialog state | Unchanged | Can modify |
| Timing | Any time | Not during pending transaction |
| Complexity | Simple | Complex (glare handling) |

## DSL API

```elixir
# Put call on hold (sends UPDATE with a=sendonly)
call |> hold()

# Resume from hold (sends UPDATE with a=sendrecv)
call |> resume()

# Low-level UPDATE for advanced use
call |> update(sdp_offer: new_sdp, headers: %{"X-Custom" => "value"})
```

## Callbacks
- handle_update(request, call) - Remote sent UPDATE
- handle_update_complete(response, call) - Our UPDATE accepted
- handle_update_failed(status, response, call) - Our UPDATE rejected

## Implementation Notes

1. UPDATE uses non-INVITE transaction (RFC 3261 Section 17.1.2/17.2.2)
2. Must contain Contact header (RFC 3311 Section 5.1)
3. Valid in :confirmed and :early dialog states
4. Fallback to re-INVITE if remote doesn't support UPDATE (check Allow header)

## SDP Direction Mapping

- :sendrecv - Normal bidirectional
- :sendonly - We send, remote on hold
- :recvonly - We receive, we're on hold
- :inactive - Completely muted

## Dependencies
- Requires: Basic dialog support (done)
- Parallel with: EPIC-DSL-001, EPIC-DSL-003

## References
- RFC 3311: SIP UPDATE Method
- RFC 3261: SIP (non-INVITE transactions)
- RFC 3264: Offer/Answer Model

title: Add UPDATE handling to DialogStatem
description:

RFC 3311 Section 5.2 - Receiving UPDATE

```elixir
# In :confirmed state
def confirmed(:cast, {:request, %Message{method: "UPDATE"} = request}, data) do
  case validate_update(request) do
    :ok ->
      notify_handler({:update_request, request}, data)
      {:keep_state, data}
    {:error, reason} ->
      send_response(request, error_code(reason), data)
      {:keep_state, data}
  end
end

# Also handle in :early state (RFC 3311 allows this)
def early(:cast, {:request, %Message{method: "UPDATE"} = request}, data)

# Handle UPDATE response
def confirmed(:cast, {:response, %Message{cseq_method: "UPDATE"} = response}, data)
```

- SDP offer/answer processed correctly
- Handler notified of UPDATE request/response

title: Implement SDP renegotiation in MediaSession
description:

```elixir
@doc """
Process a new SDP offer for an existing session (re-negotiation).
Called when receiving UPDATE or re-INVITE with new SDP.

Renegotiation Rules:
1. Cannot add new media types (must be subset of original)
2. Can change codec preference order
3. Can change direction (sendrecv -> sendonly for hold)
4. Can change connection address (ICE restart)
"""
@spec renegotiate(session_id(), String.t()) :: {:ok, String.t()} | {:error, term()}
def renegotiate(session_id, offer_sdp)

@doc """
Create a new SDP offer for renegotiation (we initiate the change).
"""
@spec create_reoffer(session_id(), keyword()) :: {:ok, String.t()} | {:error, term()}
def create_reoffer(session_id, opts \\ [])
```

- Codec changes work

- Pipeline updated to match new SDP

title: Add UPDATE support to Bridge.Handler
description:
Handle UPDATE requests/responses in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/handler.ex

```elixir
# Handle incoming UPDATE
def handle_request(%Message{method: "UPDATE"} = request, state) do
  call = lookup_call(request)
  case process_update_sdp(request, call) do
    {:ok, answer_sdp} ->
      response = Message.response(request, 200, "OK", answer_sdp)
      send_response(response)
      invoke_callback(call, :handle_update, [request])
    {:error, reason} ->
      response = Message.response(request, error_code(reason))
      send_response(response)
  end
  {:ok, state}
end

# Handle UPDATE response (we sent UPDATE)
def handle_response(%Message{cseq_method: "UPDATE"} = response, state)
```

Acceptance:
- Incoming UPDATE handled with SDP processing
- UPDATE response processed
- Handler callbacks invoked
- Error responses for invalid UPDATE
- Integration tests

title: Add hold/resume/update to Parrot.Call
Add UPDATE callbacks to InviteHandler
Add send_update to ParrotSip.UA
description:
Add UPDATE sending in apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/ua.ex

```elixir
@doc """
Send UPDATE request within an established dialog.
RFC 3311 - The Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) UPDATE Method
"""
@spec send_update(dialog_id(), String.t() | nil, map()) :: :ok | {:error, term()}
def send_update(dialog_id, sdp \\ nil, headers \\ %{})

defp build_update_request(dialog, sdp, extra_headers) do
  %Message{
    method: "UPDATE",
    uri: dialog.remote_target,
    headers: Map.merge(%{
      "from" => dialog.local_uri,
      "to" => dialog.remote_uri,
      "call-id" => dialog.call_id,
      "cseq" => next_cseq(dialog, "UPDATE"),
      "contact" => dialog.local_contact,
      "content-type" => if(sdp, do: "application/sdp", else: nil)
    }, extra_headers),
    body: sdp || ""
  }
end
```

Acceptance:
- send_update/3 builds correct UPDATE message
- SDP included when provided
- CSeq incremented properly
- Transaction created for request
- RFC 3311 compliance
- Unit tests

title: Implement UPDATE in ActionExecutor
description:
Add execution logic in apps/parrot/lib/parrot/call/action_executor.ex

```elixir
def execute(:hold, state) do
  if supports_update?(state) do
    {:ok, offer} = MediaSession.create_reoffer(state.media_session_id, direction: :sendonly)
    ParrotSip.UA.send_update(state.dialog_id, offer)
    {:ok, %{state | assigns: Map.put(state.assigns, :hold_pending, true)}}
  else
    execute(:hold_reinvite, state)  # Fallback
  end
end

def execute(:resume, state) do
  # Similar with direction: :sendrecv
end

defp supports_update?(state) do
  allow = Map.get(state.remote_capabilities, :allow, [])
  "UPDATE" in allow
end
```

Acceptance:
- hold/resume use UPDATE when supported
- Fallback to re-INVITE when not supported
- Custom UPDATE via update/2
- Pending state tracked
- Integration tests

title: B2BUA A-leg state not updated when DSL answer() is called
description:
When the DSL's answer() operation is executed via ActionExecutor, it sends 200 OK to SIP and updates call.state to :answered, but it does not update the B2BUA's A-leg state from :init to :answered.

**Impact:**
B2BUA.connect(a_leg, b_leg) fails with :leg_not_answered error because A-leg remains in :init state.

**Root Cause:**
There is a disconnect between the DSL Call state and the B2BUA Leg state. When answer() is called in a B2BUA scenario, the B2BUA should be notified to transition the A-leg to :answered state.

**Reproduction:**
1. Run: SIP_TRACE=true LOG_LEVEL=debug mix run scripts/dev/test_b2bua_simple.exs
2. Connect with pjsua: pjsua --null-audio --no-tcp 'sip:bridge@127.0.0.1:5080;transport=udp'
3. Observe server log shows:
   - [B2BUA] Failed to connect legs: :leg_not_answered
   - a_leg: state=init, direction=inbound

**Expected Behavior:**
When answer() is executed for a B2BUA session, the A-leg should transition to :answered state so that B2BUA.connect() can successfully bridge the legs.

**Possible Fix:**
Either:
1. Have ActionExecutor.execute_answer notify the B2BUA when answering in a B2BUA context
2. Have the B2BUA automatically transition A-leg to :answered when it observes the 200 OK sent
3. Require explicit B2BUA.handle_leg_event(b2bua_pid, :a_leg, {:answered, sdp}) call in the handler

title: Bidirectional WebSocket Audio Connection
description:
Add bidirectional WebSocket audio streaming to enable real-time speech-to-speech AI integrations. Callers can speak to AI services (OpenAI Realtime, ElevenLabs) via WebSocket while hearing AI responses in real-time.

## Design Documents
- Spec: specs/004-bidirectional-ws/spec.md
- Plan: specs/004-bidirectional-ws/plan.md
- Data Model: specs/004-bidirectional-ws/data-model.md
- Research: specs/004-bidirectional-ws/research.md
- Contracts: specs/004-bidirectional-ws/contracts/

## User Stories (Priority Order)
1. **US1 (P1)**: Connect Call to Speech-to-Speech AI Service - Core bidirectional audio streaming
2. **US2 (P2)**: Handle Connection Lifecycle Events - Callbacks for connect/disconnect/reconnect/fail
3. **US3 (P3)**: Control Audio Direction - Mute/unmute inbound and outbound independently
4. **US4 (P4)**: Disconnect Bidirectional Connection - Clean disconnection and resource cleanup

## Success Criteria
- <500ms audio round-trip latency (excluding AI processing)
- 100 concurrent connections per instance
- <1% frame loss under normal conditions

## Architecture
- New WsBidirectional module hierarchy in parrot_media
- DSL operations in parrot/call.ex
- Follows existing WsAudioForker patterns
close_reason: All user stories (US1-US4) and observability complete, 310 tests passing

```elixir
defmodule ParrotMedia.WsBidirectional.Config do
  @moduledoc """
  Configuration for establishing a bidirectional WebSocket connection.
  """

  @type t :: %__MODULE__{
    connection_id: String.t(),
    url: String.t(),
    headers: [{String.t(), String.t()}],
    callback_module: module() | nil,
    callback_state: term(),
    inbound_format: :pcm_16le | :pcmu | :opus,
    outbound_format: :pcm_16le | :pcmu | :opus,
    sample_rate: pos_integer(),
    buffer_size: pos_integer(),
    jitter_buffer_ms: pos_integer(),
    connect_timeout_ms: pos_integer(),
    max_retries: non_neg_integer()
  }

  @enforce_keys [:connection_id, :url]
  defstruct [
    :connection_id,
    :url,
    headers: [],
    callback_module: nil,
    callback_state: %{},
    inbound_format: :pcm_16le,
    outbound_format: :pcm_16le,
    sample_rate: 16000,
    buffer_size: 100,
    jitter_buffer_ms: 60,
    connect_timeout_ms: 5000,
    max_retries: 5
  ]

  @spec new(keyword()) :: {:ok, t()} | {:error, atom()}
  def new(opts) do
    # Implement validation per data-model.md
  end

  @spec new!(keyword()) :: t()
  def new!(opts) do
    case new(opts) do
      {:ok, config} -> config
      {:error, reason} -> raise ArgumentError, "Invalid config: \#{reason}"
    end
  end
end
```

## Validation Rules (from data-model.md)
- connection_id: non-empty string (required)
- url: starts with ws:// or wss:// (required)
- buffer_size: 1-500
- sample_rate: positive integer
- jitter_buffer_ms: positive integer
- headers: list of {string, string} tuples

title: Implement WsBidirectional.Callback behaviour
Create WsBidirectional.Connection (Fresh WebSocket callbacks)
description:
Create the Fresh WebSocket connection handler module.

## Context
Fresh is the WebSocket library used by the project. This module implements Fresh callbacks for WebSocket events.

## File to Create
`apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/ws_bidirectional/connection.ex`

## Implementation Pattern (follow WsAudioForker)
```elixir
defmodule ParrotMedia.WsBidirectional.Connection do
  @moduledoc """
  Fresh WebSocket callbacks for bidirectional audio connection.
  """

  use Fresh

  require Logger

  @impl Fresh
  def handle_connect(status, headers, state) do
    Logger.debug("[WsBidirectional] Connected: status=\#{status}")
    # Notify connector GenServer
    {:ok, state}
  end

  @impl Fresh
  def handle_disconnect(reason, state) do
    Logger.debug("[WsBidirectional] Disconnected: \#{inspect(reason)}")
    # Notify connector GenServer for reconnection
    {:ok, state}
  end

  @impl Fresh
  def handle_in({:binary, data}, state) do
    # Audio data from WebSocket - forward to connector
    {:ok, state}
  end

  @impl Fresh
  def handle_in({:text, data}, state) do
    # Text/JSON message - forward to callback module
    {:ok, state}
  end

  @impl Fresh
  def handle_info(msg, state) do
```
