title: Integrate DialogStore into DialogStatem
description:
Modify DialogStatem to persist state changes to DialogStore.

## Changes to DialogStatem

### On dialog creation (init)
```elixir
def init({:uas, request, response}) do
  {:ok, dialog} = Dialog.uas_create(request, response)
  
  # Persist to store (async broadcast to other nodes)
  DialogStore.store(dialog.id, serialize(dialog))
  
  {:ok, :confirmed, %{dialog: dialog}}
end
```

### On state changes
```elixir
def confirmed(:cast, {:uas_request, msg}, data) do
  updated_dialog = process_request(data.dialog, msg)
  
  # Persist change (async)
  DialogStore.store_async(updated_dialog.id, serialize(updated_dialog))
  
  {:keep_state, %{data | dialog: updated_dialog}}
end
```

### On termination
```elixir
def terminate(_reason, _state, data) do
  DialogStore.delete(data.dialog.id)
  :ok
end
```

### Add recovery start function
```elixir
def start_recovered(stored_state) do
  GenStatem.start_link(__MODULE__, {:recover, stored_state})
end

def init({:recover, stored_state}) do
  dialog = deserialize(stored_state)
  Registry.register(ParrotSip.Registry, dialog.id, self())
  {:ok, dialog.state, %{dialog: dialog}}
end
```

## Files to modify
- apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/dialog_statem.ex

## Async writes
Use cast to a writer process for non-critical updates to avoid blocking the state machine. Dialog creation should be sync to ensure stored before 200 OK sent.

title: Capture dialog_id in ActionExecutor after answer
description:
Modify execute_answer to capture the dialog_id from the final response and store it in the Call struct.

## Context
After Task ezy.1, Transaction.Server.response/2 returns {:ok, final_response} with the To tag added. We need to use this to compute the dialog_id.

## Changes to ActionExecutor.execute_answer

```elixir
def execute_answer(call, %{sip_msg: sip_msg} = context, _opts) do
  Logger.debug("[ActionExecutor] Executing answer operation")
  
  # Build 200 OK response
  response = Message.reply(sip_msg, 200, "OK")
  
  # Send the response - now returns final response with To tag
  {:ok, final_response} = send_response(context, response)
  
  # Compute dialog_id from final response
  # For UAS: local_tag = To tag (us), remote_tag = From tag (them)
  dialog_id = compute_dialog_id(sip_msg, final_response)
  
  # Update call state with dialog_id
  updated_call = call
    |> Map.put(:state, :answered)
    |> Map.put(:__dialog_id__, dialog_id)
  
  {:ok, updated_call}
end

defp compute_dialog_id(request, response) do
  call_id = request.call_id
  
  # From tag (remote party) from request
  remote_tag = get_in(request, [:from, :parameters, "tag"])
  
  # To tag (us) from response
  local_tag = get_in(response, [:to, :parameters, "tag"])
  
  # Generate dialog ID from UAS perspective
  ParrotSip.Dialog.generate_id(:uas, call_id, local_tag, remote_tag)
end
```

## Files to modify
- apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex

## Dependencies
- Requires ezy.1 (Transaction.Server returns final response)

## Tests
- Test dialog_id is set in Call after answer
- Test dialog_id matches expected format

title: Implement execute_hangup to send BYE via dialog
description:
Modify execute_hangup to look up the dialog and send a BYE request.

## Current Behavior
```elixir
def execute_hangup(call, %{media_pid: media_pid} = _context) do
  # Only stops media - does NOT send BYE
  if media_pid, do: send(media_pid, {:stop_media})
  updated_call = %{call | state: :terminated}
  {:ok, updated_call}
end
```

## New Implementation
```elixir
def execute_hangup(call, context) do
  Logger.debug("[ActionExecutor] Executing hangup operation")
  
  # 1. Stop media session if running
  if context.media_pid do
    send(context.media_pid, {:stop_media})
  end
  
  # 2. Send BYE if we have a dialog
  case call.__dialog_id__ do
    nil ->
      Logger.warning("[ActionExecutor] No dialog_id, cannot send BYE")
      
    dialog_id ->
      send_bye(dialog_id, call)
  end
  
  # 3. Update call state
  updated_call = %{call | state: :terminated}
  {:ok, updated_call}
end

defp send_bye(dialog_id, call) do
  # Look up dialog from store
  case ParrotSip.DialogStore.fetch(dialog_id) do
    {:ok, dialog_state} ->
      # Create BYE message using dialog
      {:ok, bye_msg, _updated_dialog} = ParrotSip.Dialog.uac_request(:bye, dialog_state)
      
      # Send BYE via client transaction
      test_pid = self()
      callback = fn result ->
        send(test_pid, {:bye_result, call.id, result})
      end
      
      {:uac_id, _trans} = ParrotSip.Transaction.Client.request(bye_msg, callback)
      
      Logger.info("[ActionExecutor] Sent BYE for call \#{call.id}")
      :ok
      
    {:error, :not_found} ->
      Logger.warning("[ActionExecutor] Dialog \#{dialog_id} not found, cannot send BYE")
      :ok
  end
end
```

## Alternative: Use DialogStatem directly
If the dialog process is still running, we could send a message to it:
```elixir
defp send_bye(dialog_id, _call) do
  case ParrotSip.DialogStatem.send_bye(dialog_id) do
    :ok -> :ok
    {:error, reason} -> Logger.warning("Failed to send BYE: \#{inspect(reason)}")
  end
end
```

This would require adding send_bye/1 to DialogStatem.

## Files to modify
- apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex

## Dependencies
- Requires ezy.4 (dialog_id captured in Call)
- Requires cz5 epic (DialogStore available)

## Tests
- Test BYE is sent when hangup called on answered call
- Test hangup works even without dialog_id (graceful degradation)
- Integration test: full call flow with BYE

```elixir
  # SIPp scenario: INVITE, expect 200, send ACK, expect BYE
```

## SIPp Scenario: uac_expect_bye.xml
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<scenario name="UAC expects server-initiated BYE">
  <!-- Send INVITE -->
  <send>
    <![CDATA[
      INVITE sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port] SIP/2.0
      Via: SIP/2.0/UDP [local_ip]:[local_port];branch=[branch]
      From: <sip:sipp@[local_ip]:[local_port]>;tag=[call_number]
      To: <sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port]>
      Call-ID: [call_id]
      CSeq: 1 INVITE
      Contact: <sip:sipp@[local_ip]:[local_port]>
      Content-Length: 0
    ]]>
  </send>
  
  <!-- Expect 200 OK -->
  <recv response="200"/>
  
  <!-- Send ACK -->
  <send>
    <![CDATA[
      ACK sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port] SIP/2.0
      Via: SIP/2.0/UDP [local_ip]:[local_port];branch=[branch]
      From: <sip:sipp@[local_ip]:[local_port]>;tag=[call_number]
      To: <sip:test@[remote_ip]:[remote_port]>[peer_tag_param]
      Call-ID: [call_id]
      CSeq: 1 ACK
      Content-Length: 0
    ]]>
  </send>
  
  <!-- Expect BYE from server -->
  <recv request="BYE"/>
  
  <!-- Send 200 OK to BYE -->
  <send>
    <![CDATA[
      SIP/2.0 200 OK
      [last_Via:]
      [last_From:]
      [last_To:]
      [last_Call-ID:]
      [last_CSeq:]
      Content-Length: 0
    ]]>
  </send>
</scenario>
```

title: Implement PCMU (G.711 μ-law) Codec Support
description:
## Problem
PCMU (Payload Type 0, G.711 μ-law) not supported. Most VoIP phones default to PCMU. Calls fail at codec negotiation.

## Root Cause
membrane_g711_plugin only implements A-law encoding/decoding. The format defines :PCMU but encoder only outputs :PCMA.

## Files to Modify
- `/deps/membrane_g711_plugin/lib/membrane_g711/lut.ex` - Add μ-law lookup tables
- `/deps/membrane_g711_plugin/lib/membrane_g711/encoder.ex` - Support :PCMU output
- `/deps/membrane_g711_plugin/lib/membrane_g711/decoder.ex` - Support :PCMU input
- `/apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/media_session.ex` - Add codec_info(:pcmu)
- `/apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/alaw_pipeline.ex` - Rename/extend for both

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Extend membrane_g711_plugin (Preferred)
1. Add μ-law LUT functions to lut.ex:
```elixir
@spec ulaw_encode(-32_768..32_767) :: 0..255
def ulaw_encode(sample) do
  # ITU-T G.711 μ-law companding
  # Formula: F(x) = sgn(x) * ln(1 + μ|x|) / ln(1 + μ), where μ = 255
  sign = if sample < 0, do: 0x80, else: 0x00
  sample = abs(sample)
  # ... μ-law compression algorithm
end

@spec ulaw_decode(0..255) :: -32_768..32_767
def ulaw_decode(byte) do
  # Inverse of μ-law
end
```

2. Modify encoder.ex to accept encoding option:
```elixir
def_options encoding: [
  spec: :PCMA | :PCMU,
  default: :PCMA,
  description: "G.711 encoding variant"
]
```

### Option B: Custom Parrot Encoder (Simpler)
Create ParrotMedia.UlawEncoder wrapping μ-law logic like TimestampPreservingG711Encoder does for A-law.

## μ-law Algorithm Reference (ITU-T G.711)
- Compression: μ = 255, 8 segments, 16 steps per segment
- Bit layout: Sign(1) + Segment(3) + Mantissa(4)
- Different from A-law: Uses logarithmic compression with μ constant

## Test Strategy
- SDP negotiation test: Offer PCMU, verify accepted
- Pipeline test: Encode/decode round-trip
- Integration test: SIPp with PCMU payload

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] μ-law encode/decode functions in LUT
- [ ] Encoder supports :PCMU output format
- [ ] codec_info(:pcmu) returns {0, "PCMU/8000", PcmuPipeline}
- [ ] SDP offers PCMU correctly (a=rtpmap:0 PCMU/8000)
- [ ] Full audio pipeline works with PCMU codec
- [ ] SIPp test with pjsua using PCMU passes

title: RFC 3261 Compliance: Critical Protocol Fixes
description:
## Overview
Fix critical RFC 3261 compliance gaps that cause call failures in production SIP environments.

## Blocking Issues
1. ACK SDP Answer Bodies missing (RFC 3261 §13.2.2.4)
2. INVITE Retransmission Race Condition (RFC 3261 §17.2.1)
3. 3xx Redirect Handling not implemented (RFC 3261 §21)
4. B2BUA State Synchronization broken

## Success Criteria
- All SIPp scenarios pass including retransmission tests
- B2BUA bridge calls work end-to-end
- Redirect scenarios work with multiple Contact headers
- ACK contains SDP answer when 2xx has SDP offer

## RFC References
- RFC 3261 Section 13.2.2.4: ACK for 2xx
- RFC 3261 Section 17.2.1: INVITE Server Transaction
- RFC 3261 Section 21: Redirect Handling

A VoIP application developer wants to collect DTMF input (such as PIN codes, menu selections, or account numbers) from callers using the Parrot DSL.

## Goal
Enable DTMF collection from DSL handlers via collect_dtmf/2 function.

```elixir
# Add to DTMF section (create new section after Recording Operations)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DTMF Collection Operations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@doc """
Starts collecting DTMF digits from the caller.

## Options

* `:max` - Maximum digits to collect (default: 20)
* `:timeout` - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30_000)
* `:terminators` - List of digits that end collection (default: [])

## Examples

    call |> collect_dtmf(max: 4, timeout: 10_000, terminators: ["#"])

"""
@spec collect_dtmf(t(), keyword()) :: t()
def collect_dtmf(%__MODULE__{} = call, opts \\ []) when is_list(opts) do
  add_operation(call, {:collect_dtmf, opts})
end
```

## Implementation
Add after execute_stop_record (around line 270):
```elixir
@doc """
Execute the `:collect_dtmf` operation.

1. Verify call is in `:answered` state
2. Verify media_pid is available
3. Send `{:collect_dtmf, opts}` to MediaSession
"""
@spec execute_collect_dtmf(Call.t(), context(), keyword()) :: execute_result()
def execute_collect_dtmf(%Call{state: state}, _context, _opts) when state != :answered do
  {:error, :invalid_state}
end

def execute_collect_dtmf(_call, %{media_pid: nil}, _opts) do
  {:error, :no_media_session}
end

def execute_collect_dtmf(call, %{media_pid: media_pid}, opts) do
  Logger.debug("[ActionExecutor] Starting DTMF collection with opts: \#{inspect(opts)}")
  send(media_pid, {:collect_dtmf, opts})
  {:ok, call}
end
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Includes Logger.debug for FR-013
- [ ] Handles :no_media_session and :invalid_state errors

title: US2: Prompt and Collect Pattern (P2)
description:
## User Story 2 - Prompt and Collect Pattern (Priority: P2)

A VoIP developer wants a convenient way to play an audio prompt and then immediately start collecting DTMF input once the prompt finishes.

## Goal
Provide prompt/3 convenience function for play-then-collect pattern.

## Independent Test
Call prompt(call, "enter-pin.wav", max: 4), verify audio plays, then verify DTMF collection begins after playback completes.

## Files to Modify
- apps/parrot/lib/parrot/call.ex - Add prompt/3 function
- apps/parrot/lib/parrot/invite_handler.ex - Export prompt/3 in macro

## Test Files
- apps/parrot/test/parrot/call_test.exs

## Implementation Pattern
prompt/3 stores collection options in :__pending_collect__ assign, then queues play operation.
Handler checks for __pending_collect__ in handle_play_complete/2 to trigger collection.

## Acceptance Scenarios
1. Given active call, When prompt(call, file, opts) called, Then audio plays and collection starts after
2. Given prompt in progress, When DTMF pressed during playback, Then digits ignored until collection activates

## Dependencies
Depends on US1 completion (uses collect_dtmf/2 internally)

title: T019: [US2] Add prompt/3 function to Parrot.Call
description:
## Task
Add prompt/3 function to apps/parrot/lib/parrot/call.ex

## Implementation
Add after collect_dtmf/2 in the DTMF Collection Operations section:
```elixir
@doc """
Plays an audio prompt then starts DTMF collection after playback completes.

This is a convenience function that combines `play/2` with deferred `collect_dtmf/2`.
The collection options are stored in assigns and must be checked in your
`handle_play_complete/2` callback.

## Options

Same as `collect_dtmf/2`:
* `:max` - Maximum digits to collect (default: 20)
* `:timeout` - Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30_000)
* `:terminators` - List of digits that end collection (default: [])

## Example

    def handle_invite(call) do
      call
      |> answer()
      |> prompt("enter-pin.wav", max: 4, timeout: 10_000)
    end

    def handle_play_complete(file, %{assigns: %{__pending_collect__: opts}} = call)
        when not is_nil(opts) do
      call
      |> assign(:__pending_collect__, nil)
      |> collect_dtmf(opts)
    end

"""
@spec prompt(t(), String.t(), keyword()) :: t()
def prompt(%__MODULE__{} = call, file, opts \\ []) when is_binary(file) and is_list(opts) do
  call
  |> assign(:__pending_collect__, opts)
  |> play(file)
end
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Function added with @doc and @spec
- [ ] Uses assign/3 to store __pending_collect__
- [ ] Uses play/2 to queue audio
- [ ] Tests T016, T017, T018 now pass (TDD green phase)

title: T025: Create DTMFDemo example handler
description:
**File**: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/examples/dtmf_demo.ex (NEW)

**Implementation**:
```elixir
defmodule Parrot.Examples.DTMFDemo do
  @moduledoc """
  Example DTMF collection handler demonstrating:
  - Basic collect_dtmf/2 usage
  - prompt/3 convenience function
  - Multi-step IVR pattern
  - PIN retry logic
  """
  use Parrot.InviteHandler

  @impl true
  def handle_invite(call) do
    call
    |> answer()
    |> assign(:step, :menu)
    |> play("priv/audio/welcome.wav")
  end

  @impl true
  def handle_play_complete("priv/audio/welcome.wav", call) do
    call |> collect_dtmf(max: 1, timeout: 5_000)
  end
  
  def handle_play_complete(_file, call), do: {:noreply, call}

  @impl true
  def handle_dtmf(digit, %{assigns: %{step: :menu}} = call) do
    case digit do
      "1" -> call |> assign(:step, :pin) |> play("priv/audio/enter-pin.wav")
      :timeout -> call |> hangup()
      _ -> call |> play("priv/audio/invalid.wav")
    end
  end
  
  def handle_dtmf(:timeout, call), do: call |> hangup()
end
```

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] File created at correct path
- [ ] Uses collect_dtmf/2 correctly
- [ ] Demonstrates multi-step IVR pattern
- [ ] Code compiles without warnings

**Dependencies**: US1, US2, US3 complete

title: Add PCMU (G.711 μ-law) codec support
description:
Add support for PCMU (G.711 μ-law, payload type 0) codec encoding/decoding.

## Background
Currently only PCMA (A-law) and Opus codecs are supported because membrane_g711_plugin only provides A-law encoding. PCMU is commonly used in North America and would improve interoperability.

## Options
1. Contribute μ-law support to membrane_g711_plugin upstream
2. Create a separate membrane_ulaw_plugin
3. Refactor into a single G711Pipeline that accepts a :law option (:a or :u)

## Affected Areas
- ParrotMedia.MediaSession (codec_info function)
- ParrotMedia.Sdp (@codec_defs)
- Pipeline selection logic
- Tests and documentation

## References
- RFC 3551: RTP Profile (PCMU = PT 0, PCMA = PT 8)
- membrane_g711_plugin: https://github.com/membraneframework/membrane_g711_plugin

title: [CODE-REVIEW] TTS Synthesizer Process Safety
description:
## Problem
The TTS Synthesizer has two critical P1 issues that together create potential production failures:

1. **Unbounded spawn_link** (`synthesizer.ex:262-287`): External API calls (OpenAI, ElevenLabs) are spawned without proper task supervision or timeout handling
2. **Infinity timeout** (`synthesizer.ex:131`): `GenServer.call(..., :infinity)` means callers wait forever if spawned task hangs

## Impact
- Memory leaks from accumulated hanging processes
- Deadlocked callers waiting forever
- Cascading call handler stalls

## Recommended Fix
- Use `Task.Supervisor` for external API calls with configurable timeout
- Replace `:infinity` with reasonable timeout (30s default, configurable)
- Add proper error handling for timeout scenarios

## Affected Files
- `apps/parrot/lib/parrot/tts/synthesizer.ex`

## Domain
Process & Concurrency, Error Handling

title: [CODE-REVIEW] Duplicate MOS Configuration
description:

1. `config/config.exs` (lines 29-39)

## Impact
- Maintenance burden - must update both files
- Risk of config drift

## Fix
Remove the duplicate from `apps/parrot_media/config/config.exs`.
Keep only root config - umbrella apps should centralize shared config.

## Severity
P2 - Tech debt, maintenance burden

title: Generate DTMF Collection DSL Tasks
description:
## Problem
DTMF Collection spec (007-dsl-dtmf-collection) exists but has 0/32 tasks complete. Spec needs task generation.

## Current State

## Required Work
1. Review spec for completeness
2. Generate tasks.md from spec
3. Prioritize tasks for MVP

## DSL Functions Needed
```elixir
# Basic collection
collect_dtmf(call, max: 4, timeout: 10_000, terminators: ["#"])

# Prompt + collect
prompt(call, "audio/enter-pin.wav", max: 4)

# TTS + collect  
say_prompt(call, "Enter your PIN", max: 4)
```

## Integration Points
- TelephoneEventParser extracts DTMF from RTP
- MediaSession dispatches events to handler
- Call.Server receives {:media_event, :dtmf, digit}
- InviteHandler.handle_dtmf/2 callback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Review spec for gaps
- [ ] Generate tasks.md with prioritized tasks
- [ ] Identify blocking dependencies
- [ ] MVP tasks clearly marked

title: [test_answer_play] AlawPipeline crashes - missing :audio_source child
description:
When running test_answer_play.exs, the AlawPipeline crashes with Membrane.UnknownChildError.

**Error:**
```
** (Membrane.UnknownChildError) Unknown child: :audio_source
Available children: [:rtp, :udp_endpoint]
```

**Root Cause:** The pipeline tries to notify :audio_source child that doesn't exist. Either:
1. The :audio_source child is not being spawned during pipeline setup
2. Or the notification code references a child that was removed

**File:** apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/pipelines/alaw_pipeline.ex (likely)

**To Reproduce:**
1. Run: ./scripts/dev/run_test.sh test_answer_play
2. Call: sip:test@127.0.0.1:5080 with pjsua
3. Observe crash in logs

title: Implement Missing DSL Operations
description:
The Parrot.Call DSL defines operations that are not yet implemented in ActionExecutor.

**Missing Operations (8 total):**

1. Recording (2):
   - :record - Start recording to file
   - :stop_record - Stop recording

2. DTMF (2):
   - :collect_dtmf - Collect DTMF digits
   - :prompt - Play audio and collect DTMF

3. Call Bridging (2):
   - :bridge - Bridge to another endpoint (B2BUA)
   - :fork - Fork to multiple endpoints

4. Media Forking (2):
   - :fork_media - Fork media stream to external destination
   - :stop_fork_media - Stop media fork

**Dependencies:**
- Recording requires MediaSession recording support
- DTMF requires MediaSession DTMF callback integration
- Bridging requires B2BUA/UAC capabilities (major feature)
- Media forking requires pipeline modifications

**Priority Order:**
1. Recording/DTMF - Core IVR functionality
2. Media forking - Common for transcription services
3. Call bridging - Major B2BUA work (separate epic)
close_reason: All DSL operations implemented - recording, DTMF, media forking complete

title: Create TelephoneEventParser element
description:
RFC 2833/4733 Telephone-Event Parser - Membrane Framework Element

**Spec**: specs/003-telephone-event-parser/spec.md
**Plan**: specs/003-telephone-event-parser/plan.md  
**Tasks**: specs/003-telephone-event-parser/tasks.md

## Status
Speckit specify complete. Ready for implementation.

## Purpose
Parse DTMF digits from RTP telephone-event packets per RFC 4733.
Emit {:dtmf, digit} notifications to parent pipeline.
Pass all RTP buffers through unchanged (filter pattern).

## Location
apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/elements/telephone_event_parser.ex

## User Stories (6)
- US1 [P1]: Detect Single DTMF Digit (MVP)
- US2 [P1]: Collect Multiple Digits in Sequence  
- US3 [P2]: Filter by Payload Type
- US4 [P2]: Support Special Keys (*, #, A-D)
- US5 [P3]: Handle Long Key Presses
- US6 [P3]: Recover from Packet Loss

## Phases (48 tasks)
1. Setup (3): Directory structure
2. Foundational (5): Core element skeleton - BLOCKS all user stories
3. US1 (8): Single digit detection - MVP
4. US2 (4): Sequential digits
5. US3 (4): Payload filtering
6. US4 (5): Special keys
7. US5 (4): Long press handling
8. US6 (5): Packet loss recovery
9. Edge Cases (4): Error handling
10. Integration (6): Pipeline tests & polish
close_reason: TelephoneEventParser implementation complete - all tests pass

title: Create WebSocket audio forking GenServer
description:
Create GenServer for forking audio to WebSocket endpoints (AI transcription services).

**BLOCKED: Requires speckit specify process before implementation**

Purpose: Stream call audio to WebSocket endpoints like Deepgram, AssemblyAI, OpenAI Realtime

Location: apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/ws_audio_forker.ex

Architecture decision: GenServer + WebSockex (not Membrane element)
- Better failure isolation (WS issues don't crash call)
- Decoupled from pipeline lifecycle
- Simpler implementation
- Standard OTP patterns

High-level requirements:
- Open WebSocket connection to configured URL
- Receive audio buffers via message passing from pipeline Tee
- Stream audio in configured format (PCM, Opus, etc.)
- Handle reconnection, backpressure, errors gracefully
- Support multiple concurrent forks per call

Common services:
- Deepgram (wss://api.deepgram.com/v1/listen)
- AssemblyAI (wss://api.assemblyai.com/v2/realtime/ws)
- OpenAI Realtime API

3. Verify media_pid is available
4. Send {:stop_record} to media_pid
5. Add defp execute_operation clause for :stop_record
6. Return {:ok, call, :continue}

title: Implement :collect_dtmf operation
description:
Implement execute_collect_dtmf/4 in ActionExecutor.

Operation: {:collect_dtmf, opts}

Options:
- :max - Maximum digits to collect
- :timeout - Timeout in milliseconds
- :terminators - List of terminating digits

Implementation:
1. Add execute_collect_dtmf/4 function
2. Verify call is in :answered state
3. Verify media_pid is available
4. Send {:collect_dtmf, opts} to media_pid
5. MediaSession will call handler's handle_dtmf_collected/2 when done
6. Add defp execute_operation clause for :collect_dtmf
7. Return {:ok, call, :continue}

MediaSession Integration:
- MediaSession must accumulate DTMF events
- Call handler when max reached, terminator pressed, or timeout
- Need to define callback in InviteHandler for DTMF result

File: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex
close_reason: Already implemented - execute_collect_dtmf exists in ActionExecutor

title: Implement :prompt operation
description:
Implement execute_prompt/4 in ActionExecutor.

Operation: {:prompt, file, opts}

Options:
- :collect - Keyword list with DTMF collection options

Implementation:
1. Add execute_prompt/4 function
2. Verify call is in :answered state
3. Verify media_pid is available
4. Send {:prompt, file, collect_opts} to media_pid
5. MediaSession plays file AND collects DTMF simultaneously
6. DTMF during playback cancels playback (barge-in)
7. Add defp execute_operation clause for :prompt
8. Return {:ok, call, :continue}

MediaSession Integration:
- Must support concurrent play + DTMF collection
- First DTMF should optionally stop playback
- Call handler with collected digits

Dependencies: prw.3 (collect_dtmf)

File: apps/parrot/lib/parrot/bridge/action_executor.ex
close_reason: Already implemented - prompt exists in Call.ex

Implementation:

4. Parse destination (host:port format)
5. Send {:fork_media, destination, opts} to media_pid

7. Return {:ok, call, :continue}

MediaSession Integration:
- Pipeline must support forking RTP stream
- Membrane has elements for this (Tee, UDP sink)
- Add fork destination to pipeline dynamically

Use Cases:
- Real-time transcription
- Call recording to external server
- AI conversation analysis

title: B2BUA Call Bridging
description: Implement full B2BUA softswitch capabilities. See docs/plans/2026-01-25-b2bua-design.md for complete design.

title: Create Parrot.Bridge.B2BUA module
description:
Create the B2BUA coordinator module.

Responsibilities:
- Coordinate A-leg and B-leg calls
- Handle state transitions
- Manage media bridging
- Route signaling between legs

State Machine States:
- :setup - Creating B-leg
- :early_media - B-leg ringing (183)
- :connected - Both legs answered
- :terminating - One leg hanging up
- :terminated - Both legs down

Key Functions:
- start_link(a_leg_call, destination, opts)
- handle_b_leg_response(b2bua, response)
- handle_bye(b2bua, leg)
- bridge_media(b2bua)

title: Implement :bridge operation in ActionExecutor
description:
Implement execute_bridge/4 in ActionExecutor.

Operation: {:bridge, destination, opts}

Options:
- :timeout - Timeout in milliseconds
- :headers - Custom headers for B-leg INVITE
- :handler - B-leg handler module

Implementation:
1. Add execute_bridge/4 function
2. Verify call is in :answered state
3. Start B2BUA.start_link(call, destination, opts)
4. Return {:ok, updated_call, :stop}

Note: Bridge is a terminal operation - execution stops here.
B2BUA manages both legs from this point.

Dependencies: q90.1 (B2BUA module)

title: Implement :fork operation in ActionExecutor
description:
Implement execute_fork/4 in ActionExecutor.

Operation: {:fork, destinations, opts}

Options:
- :strategy - :first_answer or :ring_all
- :timeout - Timeout in milliseconds

Implementation:
1. Add execute_fork/4 function
2. Verify call is in :answered state
3. Start multiple B-leg INVITEs (via B2BUA or ForkController)
4. Handle strategy:
   - :first_answer - Cancel others when one answers
   - :ring_all - Wait for all to ring before connecting
5. Return {:ok, updated_call, :stop}

Dependencies: q90.1 (B2BUA module)

title: Parrot DSL Framework
description: Phoenix-style DSL for building VoIP applications. New parrot app that sits on top of parrot_sip and parrot_media. See docs/plans/2026-01-06-parrot-dsl-design.md

title: Create parrot app scaffold
description: Create the new parrot app in the umbrella with proper deps on parrot_sip and parrot_media

title: Create parrot app with mix new
description: Run mix new apps/parrot --sup to create the new app in the umbrella

title: Add deps on parrot_sip and parrot_media
description: Update apps/parrot/mix.exs to depend on parrot_sip and parrot_media

title: Create Parrot module with child_spec
description: Create lib/parrot.ex with child_spec for application startup: {Parrot, router: MyRouter, transports: [...]}

title: Call struct and pipeline builder
description: Implement Parrot.Call struct with assigns and pipeline builder functions (answer, play, bridge, etc.)

title: Define Parrot.Call struct with assigns, from, to, method fields
description: Create the core Call struct used throughout the DSL. Must support: assigns map for per-call state, from/to URIs, method, and internal refs. See MiniPBX handlers using call.assigns pattern.

title: Implement signaling operations: answer/0, answer/1, reject/1, hangup/0
description: Pipeline ops for call signaling. answer() sends 200 OK, answer(sdp_opts) with custom SDP, reject(status) sends error response, hangup() sends BYE. Used in MiniPBX: invite |> answer() |> play()

title: Implement assign/2 for per-call state
description: Like Phoenix conn.assigns. call |> assign(:menu, :main) |> assign(:retries, 0). Used throughout MiniPBX for IVR state, caller info, recording filenames.

title: Implement playback operations: play/1, play/2
description: play(file) plays single file, play([files]) plays sequence, play(file, loop: true) loops. Triggers handle_play_complete callback when done. Used in MiniPBX auto-attendant menu.

title: Implement recording operations: record/2, stop_record/0
description: record(path, opts) with max_duration, terminators, beep options. Triggers handle_record_complete(path, duration, call). Used in MiniPBX voicemail.

title: Implement DTMF collection: collect_dtmf/1
description: collect_dtmf(max: 4, timeout: 5000, terminators: ["#"]). Triggers handle_dtmf(digits, call) or handle_dtmf(:timeout, call). Used in MiniPBX IVR menus.

title: Implement prompt/2 combining play with collect_dtmf
description: prompt(file, collect: [max: 4, timeout: 10000]) plays then collects. Triggers handle_prompt_complete(file, digits, call). Convenience combo.

title: Implement bridging: bridge/2
description: bridge(dest, timeout: 30000, headers: %{}, handler: BLegHandler). Creates B-leg, triggers handle_bridge_complete(:answered|{:failed, reason}, call). Used in MiniPBX extension calls.

title: Implement forking: fork/2
description: fork(destinations, strategy: :first_answer, timeout: 30000). Ring multiple endpoints, first answer wins. Triggers handle_fork_complete({:answered, winner}|:no_answer, call). Used in MiniPBX ring-all.

title: Router DSL
description: Phoenix-style router with scope, pipeline, plug macros and IP/header/from/to matching

title: Create use Parrot.Router macro with __using__ callback
description: Macro that sets up router module with necessary imports, module attributes for storing routes, and compiles route table at compile time. See MiniPBX.Router example.

title: Implement pipeline/2 macro for defining named pipelines
description: pipeline :authenticated do plug :verify_registration end. Stores plugs in module attribute, executed in order when pipe_through called.

title: Implement scope/2 macro with matching options
description: scope opts do ... end. Match options: from_ip (CIDR/list), from (URI pattern), to (pattern), header ({name, value}). Nested routes inherit scope conditions.

title: Implement pipe_through/1 macro
description: pipe_through :authenticated - applies named pipeline to all routes in current scope. Multiple pipe_through calls accumulate.

title: Implement invite/2 macro for INVITE routing
description: invite pattern, HandlerModule - routes matching INVITEs to handler. Patterns: "1xx" (extension range), "9~" (prefix match), "*" (catch-all). Used in MiniPBX for extension/outbound/inbound routing.

title: Implement register/1 and presence/1 macros for handler assignment
description: register MyRegistrationHandler - assigns REGISTER handler. presence MyPresenceHandler - assigns SUBSCRIBE/PUBLISH handler. Global assignments, not scoped.

title: Implement IP matching with CIDR support
description: from_ip: "192.168.0.0/16" - match source IP against CIDR. Support list: from_ip: ["10.0.0.1", "10.0.0.2"]. Used to distinguish internal vs trunk traffic.

title: Implement route dispatch function
description: Generate dispatch/1 function at compile time that pattern matches incoming requests against route table, runs plugs, returns handler module.

title: InviteHandler behaviour
description: Behaviour for call handlers with defined callbacks: handle_invite, handle_play_complete, handle_dtmf, handle_bridge_complete, etc.

title: Define Parrot.InviteHandler behaviour with @callback specs
description: Define behaviour module with @callback for all InviteHandler functions. Provides typespec documentation for implementers.

title: Implement sensible default callbacks
description: Default implementations: bridge_failed -> play tone, hangup. no_answer -> play tone, hangup. Implementers override only what they need.

title: Implement use Parrot.InviteHandler macro with defaults
description: Macro that injects @behaviour, imports pipeline ops, provides default implementations. Implementers override only what they need.

title: Implement handle_invite/1 callback routing
description: Entry point when INVITE routed to handler. def handle_invite(invite) - returns pipeline result. See MiniPBX.Handlers.ExtensionsHandler.

title: Implement handle_play_complete/2 callback
description: Called when playback finishes. def handle_play_complete(filename, call) - pattern match on filename to determine next action. Used extensively in MiniPBX IVRs.

title: Implement handle_dtmf/2 callback
description: Called when DTMF collected. def handle_dtmf(digits, call) or def handle_dtmf(:timeout, call). Pattern match on digits and call.assigns for menu state.

title: Implement handle_bridge_complete/2 callback
description: Called when bridge ends. def handle_bridge_complete(:answered, call) or {:failed, reason}. Reasons: :busy, :no_answer, :rejected. Used for voicemail fallback.

title: Implement handle_fork_complete/2 callback
description: Called when fork ends. def handle_fork_complete({:answered, winner}, call) or :no_answer. Winner contains URI of endpoint that answered first.

title: Implement handle_record_complete/3 callback
description: Called when recording finishes. def handle_record_complete(filepath, duration, call). Used in MiniPBX voicemail to save message metadata.

title: Implement handle_hangup/1 callback
description: Called when call ends. def handle_hangup(call). Cleanup, presence updates. Returns {:noreply, call}. Used in MiniPBX to update presence on call end.

title: RegistrationHandler behaviour
description: Behaviour for registration handlers: authenticate, store_binding, get_bindings. Framework handles digest auth.

title: Define Parrot.RegistrationHandler behaviour
description: Define behaviour with @callback specs for authenticate/1, store_binding/3, get_bindings/1, handle_registration_expired/1.

title: Implement use Parrot.RegistrationHandler macro
description: Macro that injects @behaviour and imports helpers. Framework handles SIP mechanics (401 challenge/response), user provides business logic.

title: Implement authenticate/1 callback
description: def authenticate(credentials) returns :ok | :error. Credentials struct has username, password, realm. See MiniPBX.Handlers.RegistrationHandler.

title: Implement store_binding/3 callback
description: def store_binding(aor, contact, expires) - called after successful auth. Store in user storage (Mnesia/ETS/DB). Trigger presence update.

title: Implement get_bindings/1 callback
description: def get_bindings(aor) - returns list of current contacts for 200 OK response. Filter expired bindings.

title: Implement handle_registration_expired/1 callback
description: def handle_registration_expired(aor) - called when registration expires. Update presence to offline, cleanup.

title: Implement 401 challenge/response flow in framework
description: Framework handles: receive REGISTER, send 401 with nonce, receive REGISTER with auth, verify digest, call authenticate callback. User never sees SIP mechanics.

title: Implement registration expiry timer management
description: Framework tracks registration expiry, calls handle_registration_expired when binding expires. Use erlang timer or gen_statem timeout.

title: PresenceHandler behaviour
description: Behaviour for presence: authorize_subscription, store_subscription, get_presence, handle_publish. Plus Parrot.Presence.notify/2 API.

title: Define Parrot.PresenceHandler behaviour
description: Define behaviour with @callback specs for authorize_subscription/2, store_subscription/1, get_subscriptions/1, get_presence/1, handle_publish/2.

title: Implement use Parrot.PresenceHandler macro
description: Macro that injects @behaviour. Framework handles SUBSCRIBE/NOTIFY/PUBLISH SIP mechanics, user provides authorization and storage.

title: Implement authorize_subscription/2 callback
description: def authorize_subscription(watcher, presentity) returns :allow | :deny | :pending. Pending for approval flows. See MiniPBX allowing internal users to watch each other.

title: Implement store_subscription/1 and get_subscriptions/1 callbacks
description: Store/retrieve subscriptions from user storage. get_subscriptions(presentity) returns list of watchers to notify on state change.

title: Implement get_presence/1 callback
description: def get_presence(presentity) returns %{status: :open|:closed, note: string}. Maps internal state (:available/:busy/:offline) to presence document.

title: Implement handle_publish/2 callback
description: def handle_publish(presentity, presence_state) - called when user publishes their state. Store in user storage.

title: Implement Parrot.Presence.notify/2 API
description: Async fire-and-forget function: Parrot.Presence.notify(presentity, %{status: :busy}). Looks up watchers, sends NOTIFY to each. Called from call handlers.

title: Implement SUBSCRIBE/NOTIFY/PUBLISH SIP flow in framework
description: Framework handles: receive SUBSCRIBE, call authorize, store subscription, send 200/202. On notify/2, lookup watchers, send NOTIFY with presence XML.

title: BLegHandler behaviour
description: Advanced B-leg control: before_invite, handle_provisional, handle_answer, handle_reject callbacks

title: Define Parrot.BLegHandler behaviour
description: Define behaviour for power users needing full B-leg control. Callbacks for INVITE manipulation, provisional handling, answer/reject, in-dialog events.

title: Implement use Parrot.BLegHandler macro
description: Macro with sensible defaults. Most users never need this - only for advanced B2BUA scenarios requiring header manipulation or early media control.

title: Implement before_invite/2 callback for INVITE manipulation
description: def before_invite(invite, state) - modify INVITE before sending. put_header, remove_header, modify_sdp. Returns modified invite.

title: Implement handle_provisional/2 callback
description: Handle 180/183 responses. Return {:ring, bleg} for ring-back, {:early_media, bleg} to connect early media to A-leg.

title: Implement handle_answer/2 and handle_reject/2 callbacks
description: handle_answer returns {:connect, bleg} to bridge media. handle_reject returns {:rejected, status, bleg}. Framework then notifies A-leg handler.

title: Implement handle_reinvite/2 and handle_bye/2 callbacks

title: Media Layer: Codec and RTCP Support
description:
## Overview
Fix critical media layer gaps that prevent interoperability with standard VoIP equipment.

## Blocking Issues
1. PCMU (G.711 μ-law) codec not supported - most VoIP phones use this
2. RTCP processing not implemented - MOS scores use placeholder values
3. RTP forwarding for B2BUA not implemented

## Success Criteria
- PCMU codec negotiation and media flow works
- RTCP reports parsed and fed to MOS calculator
- Real jitter/delay/loss values in MOS calculation
- B2BUA can bridge RTP between legs

## Technical Context
- membrane_g711_plugin only implements A-law, need μ-law
- ExRTCP library already in deps, needs integration
- MOS calculator has placeholder values (jitter=0, delay=default)

title: [CODE-REVIEW] Silent Exception Swallowing in MOS Calculator
description:
## Problem
**Location:** `apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/mos/calculator.ex:506-511`

**Anti-pattern:** `rescue _ -> :ok` + `catch _, _ -> :ok` silently swallows ALL exceptions

## Code
```elixir
defp notify_handlers(handlers, message) do
  Enum.each(handlers, fn handler_pid ->
    try do
      send(handler_pid, message)
    rescue
      _ -> :ok
    catch
      _, _ -> :ok
    end
  end)
end
```

## Issue
- `send/2` doesn't raise exceptions, so try/rescue is unnecessary
- Blanket catch masks real errors (stack overflows, OOM)
- Violates "let it crash" OTP philosophy

## Fix
Remove try/rescue entirely - `send/2` never raises:
```elixir
defp notify_handlers(handlers, message) do
  Enum.each(handlers, fn handler_pid ->
    send(handler_pid, message)
  end)
end
```

## Severity
P1 - Can mask production errors
close_reason: Removed unnecessary try/rescue - send/2 never raises

title: [CODE-REVIEW] Error Handling in Media Handler Callbacks
description:
## Problem
**Location:** `apps/parrot_media/lib/parrot_media/media_session.ex:1607-1628`

Media handler exceptions are caught and logged but session continues in unknown state.

## Code
```elixir
defp forward_to_media_handler(msg, data) do
  try do
    case data.media_handler.handle_info(msg, data.handler_state) do
      # ... handle cases ...
    end
  rescue
    e ->
      Logger.error("Error in media handler: #{inspect(e)}")
      :error  # Session continues despite error!
  end
end
```

## Impact
- Bugs in user-provided handlers are masked
- Session continues in unknown/inconsistent state
- Per OTP philosophy, should crash and let supervisor restart

## Fix
Let handler exceptions propagate:
```elixir
defp forward_to_media_handler(msg, data) do
  case data.media_handler.handle_info(msg, data.handler_state) do
    # ... handle cases - no try/rescue ...
  end
end
```

## Severity
P2 - Masks handler bugs, violates OTP principles

title: [CODE-REVIEW] Test Suite Process.sleep Timing Issues
description:
## Problem
27+ instances of `Process.sleep` in transport tests cause flaky tests and slow CI.

## Locations
- `apps/parrot_transport/test/parrot_transport/udp_listener_test.exs`: 8 instances
- `apps/parrot_transport/test/parrot_transport/tcp_listener_test.exs`: 7 instances
- `apps/parrot_transport/test/parrot_transport/tls_listener_test.exs`: 12 instances

## Anti-pattern
```elixir
test "starts and reaches :bound state" do
  {:ok, pid} = UdpListener.start_link(config)
  Process.sleep(50)  # ← Flaky timing
  assert :bound = :sys.get_state(pid) |> elem(0)
end
```

## Fix
Replace with message-based synchronization:
```elixir
test "starts and reaches :bound state" do
  {:ok, pid} = UdpListener.start_link(config)
  assert_receive {:listener_ready, ^pid}, 1000
end
```

Or use explicit GenServer.call for state confirmation.

## Severity
P1 - Impacts CI reliability and developer feedback loop
close_reason: Replaced Process.sleep with sync calls and message-based waits in UDP/TCP/TLS listener tests

title: B2BUA test scripts don't start Parrot.Registry - handle_hangup callback never invoked
description:
The B2BUA test scripts (e.g., scripts/dev/test_b2bua_simple.exs) use ParrotSip.Stack.start_link directly instead of Parrot.Supervisor. This means Parrot.Registry is never started.

**Impact:**
When BYE is received, Bridge.Handler.handle_bye/3 cannot find the Call.Server via lookup_by_call_id/1, so the handler's handle_hangup/1 callback is never invoked.

**Log Evidence:**
```
[Bridge.Handler] No Call.Server found for FBA69FB9-C6FB-4372-AAE7-110AA05C9A6D
```

**Root Cause:**
Call.Server.register_in_registry/1 silently does nothing when Parrot.Registry is not running (registry_available?() returns false). This is intentional for graceful degradation, but it means the test scripts miss testing the full hangup flow.

**Fix Options:**
1. Update test scripts to start Parrot.Registry before starting the SIP stack
2. Create a helper module that starts all required components for dev testing
3. Document this limitation in the test script comments

**Note:** This is a test infrastructure issue, not a production bug. The production path using Parrot.Supervisor would work correctly.

title: Implement 3xx Redirect Handling
description:
## Problem
3xx redirect responses are treated as rejections. handle_redirect/4 callback exists but is never invoked.

## Root Cause
UA.handle_cast/2 pattern matches code >= 300 and calls handle_rejected/4 for ALL 3xx-6xx. No separate pattern for 3xx redirects.

## Files to Modify
- `/apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/ua.ex`
  - Lines 499-519: Add new pattern match for 3xx BEFORE 4xx-6xx pattern
  - Invoke handle_redirect/4 callback

- `/apps/parrot_sip/lib/parrot_sip/ua/behaviour.ex`
  - Lines 123-146: Callback already defined, just needs invocation

## Implementation (Elixir Best Practices)
1. Add 3xx pattern match in ua.ex (INSERT BEFORE existing >= 300 pattern):
```elixir
# 3xx Redirect - MUST come before >= 300 pattern
def handle_cast(
      {:uac_response, entity_id, {:response, %Message{status_code: code} = response}},
      state
    ) when code >= 300 and code < 400 do
  
  contacts = normalize_contacts(response.contact)
  sorted_contacts = Enum.sort_by(contacts, &Contact.q/1, :desc)
  
  case state.handler_module.handle_redirect(code, response, sorted_contacts, state.handler_state) do
    {:redirect, selected_contact, new_state} ->
      # Retry with new target
      retry_with_redirect(entity_id, selected_contact, state, new_state)
    {:stop, reason, new_state} ->
      terminate_entity(entity_id, reason, state, new_state)
  end
end
```

2. Implement retry logic with loop detection:
```elixir
defp retry_with_redirect(entity_id, contact, state, handler_state) do
  entity = Map.get(state.entities, entity_id)
  
  # Check redirect count limit
  redirect_count = Map.get(entity, :redirect_count, 0)
  if redirect_count >= 5 do
    {:stop, :too_many_redirects, handler_state}
  else
    # Create new INVITE with Contact URI as Request-URI
    new_invite = rebuild_invite(entity.original_request, contact)
    new_invite = Message.put_max_forwards(new_invite, new_invite.max_forwards - 1)
    
    # Track redirect history
    entity = %{entity | redirect_count: redirect_count + 1}
    # Start new transaction...
  end
end
```

## Test Strategy
- SIPp scenario: INVITE → 302 with Contact → new INVITE to Contact
- Test multiple Contact headers with q-values
- Test loop detection (max 5 redirects)
- Test Max-Forwards decrement

## RFC Reference
RFC 3261 Section 21: Redirect classes (300-399)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 3xx responses invoke handle_redirect/4 callback
- [ ] Multiple Contact headers sorted by q-value
- [ ] Retry creates new INVITE with Contact URI
- [ ] Max-Forwards decremented on retry
- [ ] Loop detection prevents infinite redirects (max 5)
- [ ] 483 Too Many Hops if Max-Forwards reaches 0

title: [CODE-REVIEW] Missing @tag :slow on Integration Tests
description:
## Problem
15+ integration tests with Process.sleep lack @tag :slow.

## Affected Tests
- `apps/parrot_sip/test/parrot_sip/presence/server_test.exs` - All tests
- `apps/parrot_media/test/parrot_media/mos_test.exs` - Lines 106, 156, 212, 292
- `apps/parrot_media/test/parrot_media/fork_integration_test.exs` - Multiple

## Impact
- Integration tests run in default suite
- Slows down CI and local feedback loop
- Developers can't quickly run fast unit tests

## Fix
Add @tag :slow and configure mix test:
```elixir
@tag :slow
test "integration test" do
```

```bash
mix test --exclude slow  # Fast feedback
mix test --only slow     # Integration suite
```

## Severity
P2 - Developer experience issue
